================================================================================
Nationwide Agriculture Supply & Intelligence Network

Complete Product, Technology, and System Architecture Blueprint

Executive Vision

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Problem Landscape in Agriculture Markets

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Why Existing Agri Platforms Fail

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Product Architecture Overview

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Infrastructure Architecture

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Cloud Architecture Blueprint

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Release Management

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. The system leverages geospatial mapping, predictive analytics, and AI driven insights to forecast crop availability, demand patterns, and price intelligence across regional markets. Infrastructure is built on scalable cloud platforms with containerized services, managed databases, distributed caching, and automated scaling mechanisms to support millions of concurrent users. Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

Security architecture incorporates zero trust principles, encrypted data channels, identity management, and continuous monitoring to ensure platform resilience and protection against fraud or malicious activity. The overall design philosophy emphasizes reliability, scalability, farmer usability, and data intelligence to transform agricultural supply chains into transparent digital networks.

This section provides deep technical and operational analysis covering system design, user workflows, engineering architecture, and scalability planning. The platform is designed to operate as a nationwide digital agriculture ecosystem connecting farmers, buyers, logistics providers, financial services, and advisory intelligence into a single unified system. The architecture follows a modular microservices model where each capability such as supply discovery, crop intelligence, logistics orchestration, and transaction settlement operates as an independent service communicating through secured APIs and event driven pipelines. Offline first mobile architecture ensures farmers in rural regions with poor connectivity can still capture crop data, update harvest availability, and receive advisory content which syncs automatically when network connectivity becomes available. FILE: agriculture_supply_intelligence_platform_prd.docx

================================================================================
Nationwide Agriculture Supply & Intelligence Network

A comprehensive Product Requirement Document (PRD) and System Architecture Blueprint for building a nationwide agricultural supply discovery and intelligence ecosystem.

1. Vision and Mission

The platform aims to build India's first nationwide agriculture supply discovery and intelligence network.It connects farmers, FPOs, buyers, financial institutions, insurers, and government stakeholders.

The mission is to build a data-driven agriculture infrastructure that improves farmer income,creates transparent supply intelligence, and enables national-scale agricultural analytics.

2. Platform Objectives

ΓÇó Build the largest farmer network in India.ΓÇó Collect nationwide crop production intelligence.ΓÇó Enable buyers to discover supply before harvest.ΓÇó Provide tools for FPOs to manage operations.ΓÇó Enable financial institutions to assess crop production risks.

3. Core Ecosystem

The ecosystem includes:

1. Farmer Network App2. FPO Operations Platform3. Buyer Intelligence Platform4. Central Agriculture Data Engine

4. Farmer App Functional Requirements

Farmer onboarding:- Name- Phone number (OTP verification)- Village- District- State- Land area- Irrigation type

Features:- Crop declaration- Harvest planning- Price radar- Buyer inquiries- Community feed

5. Crop Declaration System

Farmers declare planted crops including:

Crop typeArea plantedExpected yieldHarvest date

This data enables national supply forecasting.

6. Harvest Availability System

Farmers can post upcoming harvest supply.Example:

Crop: OnionQuantity: 2 tonsHarvest in: 7 daysLocation: Village

This feeds the national supply intelligence system.

7. FPO Management Platform

Features:

- Farmer membership management- Procurement recording- Inventory tracking- Aggregated supply publishing- Payment tracking

8. Buyer Intelligence Platform

Buyers can search supply using filters:

CropStateDistrictHarvest windowQuantity

The platform provides aggregated supply insights.

9. Central Agriculture Data Engine

The core data infrastructure stores:

- Farmer profiles- Crop declarations- Harvest forecasts- FPO procurement data- Aggregated supply intelligence

10. Data Privacy and Access Model

Farmers: see only their own data.

FPOs: see member farmer data.

Buyers: see aggregated supply insights.

11. Revenue Model

Farmer App:Free or Γé╣100 yearly.

FPO SaaS:Γé╣2000ΓÇôΓé╣5000 per month.

Buyer Intelligence:Γé╣10,000ΓÇôΓé╣50,000 yearly subscription.

12. Technology Architecture

Frontend:Flutter or React Native

Backend:Serverless microservices

Database:Managed distributed cloud databases

APIs:REST / GraphQL

13. Security Architecture

Security layers:

ΓÇó API gateway protectionΓÇó Encryption at restΓÇó Role-based access controlΓÇó Secure authentication tokens

14. Scalability Design

System designed to support:

10M+ farmersMillions of crop declarationsReal-time analytics

Infrastructure uses auto-scaling cloud services.

15. Adoption Strategy

Key farmer incentive features:

Crop price radarDemand alertsNearby buyer discovery

These features encourage daily farmer usage.

16. Long-Term Expansion

Future capabilities:

Crop production heatmapsSupply forecastingInsurance integrationsCredit scoring for farmersGovernment agricultural analytics

2. Prioritised Improvement Areas

2.1 Improvement Themes

The 47 gaps cluster into six strategic improvement themes. Each theme represents a dimension of the platform that was under-specified in PRD v1.0 and requires the detailed treatment provided in the sections that follow.



Theme

Gaps Covered

Business Risk if Not Addressed

Data Architecture Completeness

G01

Developers blocked ΓÇö cannot build without full schema

Mobile Engineering Standards

G02, G08, G34

Technical debt from Day 1; crash risk on low-end devices

Payment & Commerce Integrity

G03, G18, G23, G39

Cannot collect revenue; platform liability on disputes

Intelligence & Content

G10-G13

Advisory engine useless without rules; farmers won't trust

Trust & Safety

G07, G17, G19, G20

Fraud risk; regulatory non-compliance; platform reputation

Launch & Growth Readiness

G28-G31, G42

Slow farmer acquisition; poor App Store visibility



2.2 Implementation Sequence

Gaps must be resolved before development of their dependent modules begins. The following sequence reflects the critical path:

Week

Gaps to Resolve

Dependency Unlocked

Week 1

G01 (schema), G02 (Flutter arch), G26 (error codes)

All backend and mobile development can start

Week 2

G04 (OTP flow), G07 (KYC flow), G08 (offline sync)

Auth module and Farm OS can proceed

Week 3

G05 (Meilisearch), G06 (chat schema), G13 (pricing)

Marketplace and advisory development

Week 4

G03 (Razorpay), G18 (commissions), G23 (subscriptions)

Revenue collection from Day 1 of marketplace

Week 5

G10-G12 (advisory rules + Telugu content)

Advisory engine live with real content

Week 6

G17 (image moderation), G19 (disputes), G20 (GSTIN)

Trust & safety features for pilot launch

Pre-launch

G27 (QA), G28 (ASO), G29 (feature flags), G31 (seed data)

App Store submission and pilot onboarding



3. Complete Database Schema (G01)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 defined only 5 of 20 required tables. The following 15 additional tables are required for a functional platform. All tables use the same conventions: UUID PKs, created_at/updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ defaults, soft deletes via deleted_at.

3.2 Index Strategy

Beyond primary keys, the following composite and partial indexes are critical for query performance at scale:

crops (pond_id, status)

Filter active crops per pond ΓÇö Farm Dashboard query

feed_logs (crop_id, logged_at)

Range queries for FCR calculation

harvest_listings (district, species, status)

Core marketplace search filter

offers (listing_id, status)

Offer count per listing badge

notifications (user_id, is_read, created_at)

Unread notification inbox sort

messages (conversation_id, created_at)

Chat pagination ΓÇö most critical index

daily_prices (species, size_count, date)

Price chart queries

advisory_alerts (farmer_id, is_acknowledged, created_at)

Alert inbox filter

posts (trending_score DESC, created_at DESC)

Community feed sort

posts (species_tag, created_at)

Species-filtered community feed



4. Flutter Project Architecture (G02)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 specified Flutter as the mobile technology but did not define project structure, state management patterns, routing strategy, or code conventions. Without this, developers will create inconsistent architectures that compound into major technical debt.



4.1 Folder Structure

The project follows a feature-first folder structure with a shared core layer. Each feature is fully self-contained with its own data, domain, and presentation layers.



lib/

Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ core/                         # Shared infrastructure

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ api/                      # Dio client, interceptors, base repo

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ auth/                     # JWT storage, refresh logic

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ cache/                    # Drift DB, Hive boxes

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ config/                   # App config, env vars, feature flags

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ error/                    # Failure classes, error handler

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ localization/             # ARB files, localization delegate

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ network/                  # Connectivity monitor, sync queue

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ router/                   # GoRouter setup, route guards

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ theme/                    # ThemeData, design tokens

Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ widgets/                  # Shared UI: AquaButton, AquaCard, etc.

Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ features/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ auth/                     # OTP login + onboarding

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ data/                 # AuthRemoteDataSource, AuthLocalDataSource

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ domain/               # AuthRepository (abstract), AuthUseCase

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ presentation/         # LoginScreen, OtpScreen, OnboardingScreen

Γöé   Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ providers/            # authProvider, otpTimerProvider

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ farm_os/                  # Farm, Pond, Crop, Feed, Mortality, Water

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ data/                 # Remote + Local data sources, DTOs

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ domain/               # Farm, Pond, Crop, FeedLog entities + repos

Γöé   Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ presentation/         # DashboardScreen, PondCard, FeedLogScreen...

Γöé   Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ providers/            # farmDashboardProvider, activeCropProvider

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ harvest_marketplace/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ input_marketplace/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ advisory/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ community/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ chat/

Γöé   Γö£ΓöÇΓöÇ notifications/

Γöé   ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ profile/

ΓööΓöÇΓöÇ main.dart                     # App entrypoint, ProviderScope



4.2 State Management Patterns

State Type

Solution

Example

Server data (async)

AsyncNotifierProvider (Riverpod 2)

farmDashboardProvider fetches + caches dashboard

Local-only UI state

StateProvider / StateNotifierProvider

Selected pond tab, form field visibility

Realtime updates

StreamProvider wrapping Supabase stream

chatStreamProvider(conversationId)

Offline sync queue

StateNotifierProvider + Drift

pendingSyncProvider ΓÇö queue of local writes to flush

Auth state

AsyncNotifierProvider with auto-dispose

currentUserProvider ΓÇö global, no dispose

Feature flags

Provider wrapping Growthbook SDK

featureFlagProvider(flagName) ΓåÆ bool



4.3 Routing Strategy (GoRouter)

Package

go_router ^13.0

Route Guard

authGuard checks currentUserProvider; redirects to /login if unauthenticated

Role Guard

roleGuard checks user.role; 403 screen for unauthorized routes

Bottom Nav

ShellRoute with 5 branches preserving individual nav stacks

Deep Links

GoRouter handles app:// scheme for push notification payloads

Error Route

/error/:code ΓÇö catches all unmatched or error routes



4.4 Offline-First Data Layer

Every feature that requires offline support follows this pattern:

1. Screen calls UseCase

2. UseCase calls Repository

3. Repository first returns Drift (local) data immediately

4. Repository then fetches from API in background

5. On success: writes to Drift, emits updated stream

6. On failure: SyncQueue stores mutation; retries on connectivity

7. Conflict resolution: server timestamp wins (last-write-wins)



4.5 Code Standards

Dart version

Dart 3.x ΓÇö sound null safety, records, patterns required

Linting

flutter_lints + custom analysis_options.yaml (strict)

Naming

snake_case files, PascalCase classes, camelCase variables

Widget size

No widget file exceeds 200 lines; extract to sub-widgets

No BuildContext across async gaps

Always check mounted before using context after await

Error handling

All AsyncNotifier states handle loading/data/error with proper UI

Testing

Each feature must have: 1 widget test + 1 unit test for repository



5. Payment & Commerce Integration (G03, G18, G23)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 mentioned Razorpay but did not specify subscription lifecycle, commission collection flow, webhook handling, or failure scenarios. This gap risks platform revenue being uncollectable.



5.1 Razorpay Configuration

Account Type

Razorpay Route (for marketplace payouts to farmers)

Required Products

Subscriptions, Payment Links, Route (Split Payments), Webhooks

Webhook Secret

Stored in AWS Secrets Manager; verified via HMAC-SHA256 signature

Test Mode

All development + staging on Razorpay Test mode; production key via env

Currency

INR only at launch; multi-currency post-Year 1

PCI Compliance

Razorpay handles all card data; platform never touches card numbers

GST on Commission

18% GST on platform commission amount; GST invoice auto-generated



5.2 Buyer Subscription Lifecycle

Event

Trigger

System Action

Notification

Trial Start

Buyer clicks 'Start Free Trial'

Create Razorpay subscription (status: trialing); record in subscriptions table

Welcome email + app banner

Trial Ending

3 days before trial_ends_at

No action ΓÇö Razorpay auto-charges

Push: 'Trial ending in 3 days ΓÇö card on file'

First Charge

Razorpay webhook: subscription.charged

Update subscription status ΓåÆ active; unlock tier features

Email: Payment receipt + invoice

Charge Failure

Razorpay webhook: subscription.payment.failed

Downgrade to free tier; set status ΓåÆ past_due

Push + email: 'Payment failed ΓÇö update card'

Retry Success

Razorpay retry succeeds

Restore tier; set status ΓåÆ active

Email: Payment recovered

Cancellation

Buyer cancels in app

Call Razorpay cancel API; set cancelled_at; maintain access until period_end

Email: Cancellation confirmed

Renewal

Razorpay webhook: subscription.charged (recurring)

Extend current_period_end; log transaction

Email: Renewal receipt

Upgrade

Buyer upgrades plan

Cancel current sub; create new sub; prorate credit

Email: Plan upgraded



5.3 Harvest Transaction Commission Flow

Commission is collected from the buyer at the point of offer acceptance. This flow uses Razorpay Payment Links to enable off-app payment without requiring buyer to store card in app initially.

Step

Actor

Action

System

1

Farmer

Accepts buyer offer

System: create transaction record (status: pending)

2

System

Generate Razorpay Payment Link

Amount = quantity ├ù final_price ├ù 1.015 (1.5% commission)

3

System

Send payment link to buyer

Push notification + SMS + in-app message in chat thread

4

Buyer

Completes payment

Razorpay webhook: payment.captured

5

System

Update transaction status ΓåÆ completed

Commission_amount stored; GST invoice generated

6

System

Release farmer contact details to buyer

Phone number shared in chat; listing marked as sold

7

System

Record commission in analytics

Platform MRR dashboard updated



5.4 Webhook Endpoint Specification

Webhook Event

Endpoint

Handler Action

payment.captured

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Complete transaction, share farmer contact, send receipts

payment.failed

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Mark offer as payment_failed; notify buyer; re-open listing

subscription.charged

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Activate/renew subscription tier for user

subscription.payment.failed

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Downgrade to free; start dunning email sequence

subscription.cancelled

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Set cancellation state; schedule access removal

refund.processed

POST /webhooks/razorpay

Update transaction to refunded; restore listing if within 24h



Security Note

All incoming webhook requests must be verified: compute HMAC-SHA256(webhook_body, RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET) and compare with X-Razorpay-Signature header. Reject any request that fails verification with HTTP 401.



6. Search Architecture ΓÇö Meilisearch (G05)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 specified Meilisearch but did not define indexes, ranking rules, synonyms, or the sync pipeline from the primary database. Without this, the marketplace search will be slow and inaccurate.



6.1 Index Definitions

Index: harvest_listings

Primary Key

id

Searchable Attributes

species, district, mandal, farmer_name (display), notes

Filterable Attributes

species, district, size_count, status, is_urgent, harvest_date (unix timestamp)

Sortable Attributes

harvest_date, created_at, quantity_kg, asking_price

Ranking Rules

sort ΓåÆ words ΓåÆ typo ΓåÆ proximity ΓåÆ attribute ΓåÆ exactness

Typo Tolerance

enabled, minWordSizeForTypos: 5

Displayed Attributes

id, species, size_count, quantity_kg, harvest_date, district, asking_price, is_urgent, farmer_is_verified, image_url

Sync Strategy

PlanetScale webhook ΓåÆ Lambda ΓåÆ Meilisearch update on every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE



Index: products

Primary Key

id

Searchable Attributes

name, brand, category, description

Filterable Attributes

category, species_compatibility, crop_stage, supplier_district, is_active, is_featured

Sortable Attributes

offer_price, average_rating, view_count

Synonyms

'kaduu' ΓåÆ 'feed', 'maatallu' ΓåÆ 'medicine', 'bijam' ΓåÆ 'seed' (Telugu synonyms)

Displayed Attributes

id, name, brand, category, mrp, offer_price, image_url, supplier_name, average_rating



Index: community_posts

Primary Key

id

Searchable Attributes

body, author_name

Filterable Attributes

species_tag, language, tags, moderation_status

Sortable Attributes

trending_score, created_at, like_count

Displayed Attributes

id, body (truncated 200 chars), author_name, species_tag, like_count, created_at



6.2 Database ΓåÆ Meilisearch Sync Pipeline

Trigger

Mechanism

Latency

New harvest listing created

POST /v1/harvest-listings Lambda calls Meilisearch SDK immediately after DB insert

< 500ms

Listing status update

PATCH handler calls Meilisearch update after DB update

< 500ms

Product added/updated

Supplier product Lambda calls Meilisearch update

< 500ms

Community post created/moderated

Post Lambda calls Meilisearch update

< 500ms

Full re-index (recovery)

Scheduled Lambda daily 2 AM ΓÇö reads DB, rebuilds all indexes

< 5 min

Index health check

UptimeRobot pings GET /health on Meilisearch every 5 min

N/A



7. Chat & Realtime Architecture (G06)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 mentioned WebSockets and Supabase Realtime but did not specify channel naming, message schema, read receipt system, or media sharing flow.



7.1 Supabase Realtime Channel Strategy

Channel naming

conversation:{conversation_id}

Auth

Supabase RLS policy: only users in conversation.participant_ids can subscribe

Events broadcast

INSERT on messages table auto-broadcast to channel subscribers

Connection management

Flutter app subscribes on chat screen open; unsubscribes on pop

Reconnection

Supabase SDK handles reconnection with exponential backoff automatically

Presence

channel.track({ user_id, is_typing }) for typing indicators



7.2 Message Types & Metadata Schemas

type

metadata JSON Structure

Rendered As

text

{}

Plain text bubble

image

{ url: string, width: int, height: int, thumbnail_url: string }

Tappable image with lightbox

offer_update

{ offer_id, event: 'submitted|accepted|rejected|countered', price, quantity }

Highlighted offer card in chat

system

{ event: 'listing_sold|listing_expired|contact_shared', body_te: string, body_en: string }

Grey system message divider



7.3 Image Sharing in Chat

Step

Action

Detail

1

User selects image

Flutter image_picker; max 5MB; compressed to 800px, JPEG 80%

2

Upload to R2

Direct upload via pre-signed URL from POST /v1/chat/media/upload-url

3

Send message

POST /v1/conversations/{id}/messages with type: image + media_url

4

Thumbnail generation

Lambda triggered on R2 upload; generates 200px thumbnail; stores as media_url + _thumb

5

Display

cached_network_image shows thumbnail; full image on tap



7.4 Read Receipts

Read receipts use the is_read_by JSON column on messages. When a user opens a conversation, the app sends PATCH /v1/conversations/{id}/read which batch-updates all unread messages in that conversation. The response updates is_read_by for the requesting user.

Single tick

Message created_at shown ΓÇö message sent

Double tick (grey)

All participant user_ids are keys in is_read_by ΓÇö message delivered

Double tick (teal)

All participant user_ids have non-null timestamps in is_read_by ΓÇö read

API

PATCH /v1/conversations/{id}/read ΓÇö marks all messages as read by auth user



8. KYC & Trust Verification (G07, G20)

8.1 Farmer KYC Flow

KYC is optional for farmers at registration but required to: (a) receive payment for harvests, (b) unlock 'Verified Farmer' badge on listings, (c) access credit products in future. KYC is triggered via a prominent in-app prompt after the first crop cycle is created.



Step

Screen

Action

System

1

KYC Intro

Explains benefits of verification; shows document types accepted

Render with benefits list; skip option available

2

Document Selection

Farmer selects doc type (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID)

Guides to appropriate next screen

3

Document Capture

Camera capture of front + back; or gallery upload

flutter_camera with rectangle overlay; max 5MB; JPEG

4

Selfie Capture

Selfie with document held next to face

Liveness check guide; send to R2

5

Upload & Submit

Documents uploaded to R2; kyc_documents record created (status: pending)

POST /v1/kyc/submit; admin notified

6

Admin Review

Admin reviews in admin panel; checks doc clarity + name match

Approve (user notified) or Reject (reason shown to user)

7

Outcome

User notified via push + in-app

Approved: badge applied; Rejected: reason + retry option



8.2 Supplier GSTIN Verification

Supplier GSTIN is verified against the GST public API (taxpayerapi.gst.gov.in) by the admin during the supplier review workflow. The admin panel displays the API response alongside the supplier-submitted information.

API Endpoint

GET https://taxpayerapi.gst.gov.in/ext/api/taxpayer/{gstin}

Auth

API key from GST developer portal (free for registered taxpayers)

Checks

Business name match (fuzzy), status = Active, registration state = AP

Auto-flag

If name mismatch > 30% Levenshtein distance ΓåÆ flag for manual review

Record

gstin_verified=true + gstin_verified_at stored on supplier approval

Fallback

GST API is often down; admin can manually override with document proof



8.3 Trust Signals on Platform

Signal

Earned By

Displayed On

Verified Farmer Badge

Completed KYC + 1 completed crop cycle

Harvest listings, community profile

Experienced Farmer Badge

3+ completed crop cycles on platform

Community posts, profile

Verified Supplier Badge

GSTIN verified + admin approved

Product listings, supplier profile

Trusted Buyer Badge

3+ completed transactions + no disputes

Offer cards (farmer sees on incoming offer)

Expert Badge

Platform-assigned to qualified advisors

Community posts, advisory responses

Response Rate

Supplier % of inquiries responded to in 48h

Supplier profile

Listing Accuracy Score

Ratio of listings where final sale matches listing description

Hidden; used by algorithm for ranking



9. Offline Sync & Conflict Resolution (G08)

CRITICAL GAP

PRD v1.0 described offline-first intent but did not specify the sync queue structure, conflict resolution algorithm, or edge cases (e.g. farmer logs feed while offline; server already has a later entry from another device).



9.1 Sync Queue Schema (Local Drift Table)

Column

Type

Description

id

INTEGER AUTOINCREMENT PK

Local queue entry ID

operation

TEXT (CREATE|UPDATE|DELETE)

CRUD operation type

entity_type

TEXT

Entity name (feed_log, mortality_log, water_log, crop)

entity_id

TEXT (UUID)

Local UUID of the entity (generated client-side)

payload

TEXT (JSON)

Full serialized entity data

retry_count

INTEGER DEFAULT 0

Number of sync attempts

last_error

TEXT NULLABLE

Last error message for debugging

created_at

INTEGER (Unix ms)

Entry timestamp ΓÇö used for ordering

synced_at

INTEGER NULLABLE

Null until successfully synced



9.2 Sync Lifecycle

State

Trigger

Action

Offline write

User performs action while no connectivity

Write to Drift; add to sync_queue; show 'Saved locally' chip

Connectivity restored

ConnectivityMonitor emits connected event

Start SyncWorker; process queue in FIFO order

Sync attempt

SyncWorker dequeues item

POST/PATCH to API with entity payload

Sync success

API returns 200/201

Remove from queue; update local Drift record with server response

Sync failure (4xx)

API returns 400/409

Log error; increment retry_count; if retry_count > 3 ΓåÆ move to dead_letter_queue; notify user

Sync failure (5xx/network)

API unreachable

Exponential backoff: 30s, 2min, 10min, 1h; keep in queue

Conflict (409)

Server rejects due to state mismatch

Apply server-wins policy; update local record with server state; show 'Sync updated' toast



9.3 Client-Side UUID Generation

All entities created offline use client-generated UUIDs (UUID v4 via the dart:uuid package). When synced, the server accepts the client-provided UUID as the primary key. The API validates that the UUID does not already exist before creating. This enables true offline-first creation without ID conflicts.



9.4 Data Freshness Indicators

Green sync dot

Data confirmed synced with server within last 10 minutes

Amber cloud icon

Data saved locally; pending sync (connectivity issue)

Red exclamation

Sync failed after 3 retries; user action required

'Updated X min ago'

Last successful sync timestamp shown on dashboard



10. DPDP Act Compliance ΓÇö Data Export & Deletion (G09)

CRITICAL GAP

India's DPDP Act 2023 creates legal obligations for data subject rights. PRD v1.0 mentioned compliance but did not spec the actual user-facing flows, data package format, or deletion cascade logic.



10.1 Data Export Flow

User Entry Point

Profile ΓåÆ Privacy Settings ΓåÆ 'Download My Data'

Request Processing

Async Lambda job queued; user notified by push when ready (typically < 30 min)

Package Format

ZIP file containing JSON files per data category

Package Contents

profile.json, farms.json, ponds.json, crops.json, feed_logs.json, harvest_listings.json, transactions.json, messages.json, notifications.json

Delivery

Secure pre-signed R2 download URL (expires 24h); sent via SMS + push

Retention

Export package deleted from R2 after 24h

Frequency Limit

Maximum 1 export request per 30 days per user

API

POST /v1/privacy/data-export ΓåÆ 202 Accepted; GET /v1/privacy/data-export/status



10.2 Account Deletion Flow

Step

Timing

Action

Request

Immediate

User taps 'Delete Account' ΓåÆ confirmation modal with consequences listed

Cooling Off

0ΓÇô30 days

Account status = deletion_pending; user can cancel within 30 days; cannot create new content

Pre-deletion notice

Day 27

Push + SMS: 'Your account will be deleted in 3 days. Tap to cancel.'

Hard delete

Day 30

Lambda job executes deletion cascade

Deletion cascade

Day 30

Users: anonymise name ΓåÆ 'Deleted User', phone ΓåÆ null; Farms/ponds/crops: retain for analytics (farmer_id ΓåÆ null); Harvest listings: expire and remove; Messages: redact body ΓåÆ '[Deleted]'; Notifications: delete; KYC docs: delete from R2; Payments: retain for 7 years (tax compliance)

Confirmation

Day 30

SMS to original phone: 'Your AquaOS account has been deleted.'

API

POST /v1/privacy/delete-account ΓåÆ 202 Accepted; DELETE /v1/privacy/delete-account (cancel)

11.2 Vannamei Growth Curve Model

The platform uses the Pienaar-Thomson growth curve equation, calibrated for AP vannamei conditions. This drives harvest weight estimates shown on the farm dashboard and harvest listing suggestions.

Growth Equation

W(t) = 0.00001 ├ù L(t)^3.05 where L(t) = LΓê₧ ├ù (1 - e^(-K(t-t0)))

Parameters (AP calibration)

LΓê₧ = 195mm, K = 0.025/day, t0 = -20 days

Expected size at Day 90

~18ΓÇô20g (50ΓÇô55 count/kg)

Expected size at Day 120

~25ΓÇô28g (35ΓÇô40 count/kg)

Survival correction

Weight estimate ├ù (current estimated survival % / 100)

Display on dashboard

'Est. size: ~30 count (Day 95)' + '┬▒15% estimate' disclaimer

Recalibration

Model will be updated quarterly using actual transaction data from platform

12. Telugu Advisory Content Templates (G12)

All advisory messages must be written by a native Telugu speaker with aquaculture domain knowledge, reviewed by a fisheries expert, and tested with 5 target farmers before production deployment. The following are reference templates to guide content production.



Content Requirement

40 advisory message templates are required (1 per rule ├ù 2 severity variants ├ù language). This document provides 10 examples. Full 40-message pack must be produced by Telugu content writer as a separate deliverable before Sprint 5.
