12.2 Content Production Checklist

Hire Telugu content writer with aquaculture background (or partner with fisheries extension officer)

Draft all 40 messages in Telugu first; English translation second

Expert review by AP State Fisheries Department officer or academic

Farmer readability test: 5 farmers of varying literacy levels

Regional dialect check: Godavari Telugu vs Nellore Telugu vocabulary differences

Length validation: title Γëñ 50 chars Telugu; body Γëñ 250 chars Telugu (fits push notification)

Message must include: 1 specific observation, 1 specific action, 1 expected outcome



13. Pricing Intelligence Architecture (G13)

13.1 Data Source Strategy

Market price data for the daily price feed must come from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and resilience. AquaOS uses a hybrid approach combining web scraping, manual input, and eventually platform transaction-derived pricing.



Source

Data Available

Frequency

Method

Phase

Nellore Shrimp Market Reports

Spot prices for vannamei by count, daily

Daily 8 AM

Puppeteer Lambda scraper ΓåÆ parse HTML ΓåÆ store

Phase 1

MPEDA Weekly Reports

Export prices by species/grade

Weekly

PDF extraction Lambda via pdf-parse

Phase 1

NCS (National Cold Storage) boards

Farm gate prices Bhimavaram

3├ù weekly

Scraper + fallback: manual entry by field agent

Phase 1

Kakinada Fish Market

Fish species daily prices

Daily

WhatsApp API: field agent sends photo ΓåÆ OCR Lambda

Phase 2

Platform transactions (derived)

Actual farm-gate prices from accepted offers

Real-time

Analytics event on offer.accepted ΓåÆ price store

Phase 1 (partial)

Supplier product prices

Input cost trends (feed, medicine)

On update

Product listing update event

Phase 2



13.2 Scraper Lambda Architecture

Runtime

Node.js Lambda with Puppeteer (headless Chrome via chromium-lambda layer)

Schedule

AWS EventBridge rule: 0 8 * * * (8 AM IST daily)

Error handling

If scrape fails: use yesterday's price + add (stale) flag; alert admin

Anti-scraping

Randomised user-agent, 5ΓÇô15s delays between requests, max 3 retries

Data validation

Price sanity check: reject if > 50% change from 7-day moving average

Manual override

Admin panel allows manual price entry to correct scrape errors

Storage

daily_prices table + Upstash Redis cache (1h TTL for /pricing/daily endpoint)



13.3 Price Alert Configuration

Alert trigger

Price change > 10% from previous day for a species + size combination

Farmer targeting

Alert sent to farmers with active crops of that species

Buyer targeting

Alert sent to buyers with saved searches matching that species/district

Channel

Push notification (opted-in users) + in-app price banner

Delivery

8:30 AM daily if prices have been updated; never sent without fresh data



14. Referral Program Mechanics (G14)

14.1 Referral Flow

Step

Actor

Action

System

1

Farmer A

Taps 'Invite Friends' in Profile

Display referral_code + pre-filled WhatsApp share message in Telugu

2

Farmer B

Receives WhatsApp message; downloads app

Play Store link includes referral_code as UTM parameter

3

Farmer B

Enters referral code at registration (or auto-detected from UTM)

System validates code; creates referrals record (status: pending)

4

Farmer B

Creates first crop cycle

System checks: qualification_event = first_crop; status ΓåÆ qualified

5

System

Reward distributed

Farmer A: 30-day premium advisory unlock; Farmer B: 30-day premium advisory unlock

6

System

Referral chain limited

Max 3 reward levels deep; no pyramid scheme; no cash rewards at launch



14.2 Referral Rewards (Phase 1)

Referrer reward

30-day 'Advisory Pro' unlock (premium alert frequency + advisor chat)

Referred reward

30-day 'Advisory Pro' unlock

Maximum rewards per user

20 referrals (20 months of Advisory Pro) ΓÇö capped to prevent gaming

Reward delivery

Automatic on qualification event; push notification to both parties

Cash rewards

Not at Phase 1 ΓÇö Phase 2 consideration pending economics review



14.3 Referral Analytics

Farmer's referral dashboard

Shows: code, total referred, qualified count, rewards earned

Admin view

Referral network graph; top referrers; suspicious patterns (same IP, bulk)

Fraud prevention

Same device registrations via referral: 24h cooling period before qualifying



15. Community Post Ranking Algorithm (G16)

15.1 Trending Score Formula

The trending_score column on posts is recalculated every 30 minutes by a scheduled Lambda. The formula is inspired by Hacker News score but adapted for an agricultural community:



trending_score = (likes ├ù 2 + comments ├ù 3 + shares ├ù 5 + views ├ù 0.1) / (hours_since_post + 2)^1.5



Likes weight

2 ΓÇö basic engagement signal

Comments weight

3 ΓÇö stronger signal; conversation = quality content

Shares weight

5 ΓÇö strongest signal; WhatsApp share = high trust recommendation

Views weight

0.1 ΓÇö weak signal; reduces gaming by view inflation

Time decay exponent

1.5 ΓÇö moderate decay; older posts fall off without new engagement

Pinned posts

Pinned posts always appear first regardless of score; max 3 pinned

New post boost

Posts < 2 hours old get trending_score ├ù 1.5 multiplier for discovery



15.2 Feed Curation Logic

Default feed sort

Trending score descending (community tab)

Species filter

Filter by farmer's active crop species (personalisation)

District filter

Boost posts from same district ├ù 1.3 score multiplier

Language filter

Telugu-first; mixed feed allowed; no English-only posts appear by default

Verified boost

Posts from Verified/Experienced farmers ├ù 1.2 multiplier

Report penalty

Each report reduces score by 15% (max -50% from reports; full hide at 5 reports)

Recalculation frequency

Every 30 minutes (Lambda cron); immediate recalc on new comment/like



16. Image Moderation Pipeline (G17)

16.1 Upload ΓåÆ Moderation ΓåÆ Display Flow

Step

Where

Action

Latency

1

Client

Flutter compresses image: max 1200px long edge, JPEG 85%, max 2MB

< 1s device

2

API

POST /v1/media/upload-url returns pre-signed R2 PUT URL (60s expiry)

< 200ms

3

Client

Client PUTs image directly to R2 (bypasses API servers ΓÇö cost saving)

< 3s on 4G

4

R2

R2 triggers webhook to Lambda on object creation

< 500ms

5

Lambda

AWS Rekognition DetectModerationLabels called on image

< 1.5s

6

Lambda

If NSFW score > 70%: quarantine image + alert admin; if unclear 40ΓÇô70%: pending_review; if < 40%: approve

< 200ms

7

API

Image metadata stored: { url, status: approved|pending|quarantined, width, height }

< 100ms

8

Client

Client polls GET /v1/media/{id}/status; shows spinner until approved

Typically < 5s total

9

Display

Approved images displayed; quarantined images replaced with placeholder

Immediate



16.2 Rekognition Configuration

API

AWS Rekognition: DetectModerationLabels

MinConfidence

40% ΓÇö flag for review; 70% ΓÇö auto-quarantine

Categories blocked

Explicit Nudity, Violence, Visually Disturbing, Gambling (all auto-quarantine)

Cost

$0.001 per image; free tier: 5,000 images/month

Fallback

If Rekognition unavailable: images set to pending_review; admin reviews manually

Admin review queue

Admin panel shows all pending_review images with approve/reject action

Harvest listing photos

Lower risk; only Explicit Nudity auto-quarantined; others flagged for review



17. Dispute Resolution Workflow (G19)

17.1 Dispute Initiation

Disputes can be raised by either farmer or buyer within 7 days of a completed transaction. The dispute system uses in-app evidence collection to minimise platform liability while enabling fair resolution.



Step

Actor

Action

Timeline

1

Buyer or Farmer

Taps 'Report Issue' on completed transaction

Within 7 days of completion

2

Platform

Transaction status ΓåÆ in_dispute; both parties notified

Immediate

3

Complainant

Submits: issue category, description (max 500 chars), up to 5 photos as evidence

Within 24h of raising dispute

4

Respondent

Receives notification; can submit counter-evidence: description + photos

Within 48h of complaint

5

Platform (auto)

Chat transcript from negotiation thread attached automatically as evidence

Immediate

6

Admin

Reviews evidence in admin dispute panel; makes resolution decision

Within 5 business days (SLA)

7

Resolution

Admin selects: Full refund / Partial refund (% specified) / No action + reason

Within 5 business days

8

Execution

If refund: Razorpay refund API triggered; if no refund: decision communicated

Within 2 business days of resolution

9

Appeal

Both parties have 3 days to appeal; one appeal per dispute allowed

Within 3 days of resolution



17.2 Dispute Categories

Quality Mismatch

Harvested shrimp size/quality differs significantly from listing

Quantity Shortage

Actual quantity delivered less than agreed

No Show

Farmer unavailable at agreed harvest date

Payment Not Received

Farmer claims payment not received (despite platform record)

Specimen Issue

Diseased or dead shrimp delivered

Communication Breakdown

Buyer/farmer unresponsive after offer acceptance



17.3 Dispute Prevention Design

Harvest listings require photos ΓÇö visual baseline reduces 'quality mismatch' disputes

In-app chat is the record ΓÇö all negotiation terms visible and timestamped

System message on offer acceptance confirms: species, size, quantity, date, price ΓÇö creates agreed record

Payment via platform (not cash) creates payment proof eliminating payment disputes

Verified farmer badge requires crop cycle data ΓÇö reduces fake listing risk

18.2 Critical E2E Test Journeys (Maestro)

Journey 1 ΓÇö New Farmer: Download ΓåÆ Register (OTP) ΓåÆ Create farm ΓåÆ Add pond ΓåÆ Start crop ΓåÆ Log feed ΓåÆ View dashboard

Journey 2 ΓÇö Harvest Sale: Create listing ΓåÆ Buyer searches ΓåÆ Buyer submits offer ΓåÆ Farmer accepts ΓåÆ Payment link ΓåÆ Transaction complete

Journey 3 ΓÇö Advisory: Trigger mortality log > threshold ΓåÆ Advisory alert generated ΓåÆ Push delivered ΓåÆ Farmer acknowledges

Journey 4 ΓÇö Offline Mode: Disable network ΓåÆ Log feed ΓåÆ Re-enable network ΓåÆ Verify sync to server

Journey 5 ΓÇö Supplier: Register supplier ΓåÆ List product ΓåÆ Farmer inquiry ΓåÆ Supplier responds ΓåÆ Lead converted

18.4 Performance Test Specification (k6)

Smoke test

10 virtual users, 1 minute ΓÇö basic sanity

Load test

500 VU ramp 0ΓåÆ500 over 5 min, hold 10 min ΓÇö normal load

Stress test

1,000 VU ramp over 10 min ΓÇö find breaking point

Spike test

Instant 2,000 VU for 1 min ΓÇö simulate viral moment

Soak test

200 VU for 2 hours ΓÇö memory leaks and connection pool exhaustion

Acceptance criteria

p95 response < 800ms at 500 VU; error rate < 0.1%

21. Disaster Recovery & Security Testing (G24, G47)

21.1 Disaster Recovery Targets

Scenario

RTO

RPO

Recovery Procedure

Lambda service disruption

< 5 min

0

AWS auto-heals Lambda; API Gateway returns 503 during outage; retry at client

PlanetScale primary failure

< 2 min

< 30 sec

PlanetScale auto-failover to read replica; client retries 3├ù before error screen

Cloudflare R2 outage

< 30 min

0

Images display cached versions; uploads queued locally; manual review post-recovery

Supabase Auth outage

< 10 min

0

Existing JWT tokens remain valid (15 min TTL); no new logins until recovery; banner shown

Complete AWS region failure

< 4 hours

< 1 hour

Redeploy CDK stack to ap-south-1 (Mumbai backup region); DNS failover via Cloudflare

Data corruption event

< 8 hours

< 24 hours

Restore PlanetScale to last backup (daily automated); replay CloudWatch logs

Security breach

< 2 hours (contain)

N/A

Rotate all secrets; revoke all JWTs; notify affected users; 72h CERT-In report



21.2 Backup Strategy

PlanetScale automated backup

Daily at 2 AM IST; retained 7 days (free tier) / 30 days (Pro)

R2 object versioning

Enabled on media bucket; 30-day version retention

Infrastructure (CDK)

Git repository is the backup; re-deploy from code at any time

Secrets

AWS Secrets Manager stores all secrets; access audit logged

Export to S3

Weekly Lambda exports full DB to encrypted S3 Glacier for long-term retention

22. API Error Codes & Rate Limits (G25, G26)

22.1 Error Response Format

22.2 Error Code Registry

Code

HTTP Status

Description

Client Action

AUTH_INVALID_TOKEN

401

JWT expired or malformed

Refresh token; if fails, re-login

AUTH_INSUFFICIENT_ROLE

403

Role does not have permission for this resource

Show 'Access denied' screen

OTP_INVALID

400

OTP does not match or expired

Show error; allow resend after 60s

OTP_RATE_LIMIT

429

Too many OTP requests for this phone

Show cooldown timer

FARM_NOT_FOUND

404

Farm ID does not exist or not owned by user

Navigate back; show toast

POND_ACTIVE_CROP

409

Cannot delete pond with active crop

Prompt: harvest or abandon crop first

CROP_ALREADY_ACTIVE

409

Pond already has an active crop

Show current active crop; offer to view

LISTING_EXPIRED

410

Harvest listing has expired

Prompt: create new listing

OFFER_ALREADY_ACCEPTED

409

Another offer on listing was accepted

Show listing closed screen

OFFER_EXPIRED

410

Offer exceeded 24h window

Notify: offer expired; create new

SUPPLIER_NOT_VERIFIED

403

Supplier profile pending admin approval

Show verification pending screen

SUBSCRIPTION_REQUIRED

402

Feature requires active subscription

Show subscription upgrade screen

RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED

429

Too many requests

Retry-After header; exponential backoff

MEDIA_TOO_LARGE

413

Upload exceeds 5MB limit

Client should compress before retry

MEDIA_TYPE_INVALID

415

Unsupported file type (not JPEG/PNG)

Show supported formats

VALIDATION_ERROR

400

Request body fails schema validation

field property identifies invalid field

DUPLICATE_SUBMISSION

409

Idempotency-Key already processed

Return original response from cache

INTERNAL_ERROR

500

Unexpected server error

Show generic error; log to Sentry

SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE

503

Downstream service (DB/cache) unavailable

Retry after 30s; check status page

23.2 Low-End Device Optimisation

Optimisation

Implementation

Impact

Image lazy loading

cached_network_image with memCacheWidth 400px

ΓêÆ60% memory on image-heavy screens

List virtualisation

ListView.builder (never ListView with children array)

Smooth scroll on 2GB RAM devices

Animation reduction

MediaQuery.reduceMotion check; disable parallax/complex animations

Prevents jank on GPU-constrained devices

APK split by ABI

flutter build apk --split-per-abi

arm64-v8a APK Γëê 15MB vs 30MB universal

Image precompilation

flutter build with --no-shrink; deferred loading for feature modules

Faster cold start

Local DB pagination

Drift queries with LIMIT/OFFSET; never load all rows

Prevents OOM on 2GB devices

Background task budget

WorkManager battery-aware scheduling; avoid wake locks

Preserves battery on budget phones

Network timeout

Dio timeout: 15s connect, 30s receive; cancel on screen exit

Prevents hanging on 2G edge cases

25. BNPL, Webhooks & GST Invoices (G38, G39, G45)

25.1 Enterprise Buyer Webhook System

Enterprise buyers (Γé╣25,000/month tier) require webhook notifications to integrate AquaOS data into their internal procurement systems.



Webhook Events

new_listing_matched (saved search match), offer_accepted, listing_expired

Registration

Enterprise buyers register webhook URL + secret in Buyer Dashboard settings

Delivery

HTTPS POST with JSON payload + HMAC-SHA256 signature header

Retry

3 retries with 5 min, 30 min, 2h intervals on non-2xx response

Payload Format

{ event, timestamp, data: { listing | offer | transaction } }

Verification

Same signature pattern as Razorpay webhook; buyer verifies with their registered secret

Dead letter

Failed webhooks after 3 retries logged; buyer notified via email



25.2 GST Invoice Generation for Platform Commission

Step

Action

Details

1

Transaction completed

System records commission_amount + buyer details

2

Invoice generation

Lambda generates GST-compliant invoice as PDF

3

Invoice fields

Invoice number (sequential), date, AquaOS GSTIN, buyer GSTIN, service description, commission amount, CGST 9% + SGST 9%, total

4

Storage

PDF uploaded to R2 at invoices/{year}/{month}/{invoice_number}.pdf

5

Delivery

Link sent to buyer via email; downloadable from buyer dashboard

6

Monthly statement

Batch invoice PDF with all monthly transactions; auto-emailed on 1st of month



25.3 BNPL (Buy Now Pay at Harvest) ΓÇö Architecture Outline

Input credit (BNPL) is a Phase 2 feature requiring NBFC partnership. This section outlines the technical architecture to build toward.

NBFC Partner

Avanti Finance or Samunnati (aquaculture-focused NBFCs)

Eligibility

Farmer has 1+ completed crop cycles on platform + KYC verified

Credit Assessment

Platform provides: FCR history, survival rates, yield consistency, transaction history via API to NBFC

Loan Flow

Farmer selects product ΓåÆ taps 'Buy on Credit' ΓåÆ NBFC API pre-approves in < 30s ΓåÆ purchase proceeds ΓåÆ loan created

Repayment

At harvest: platform deducts loan amount from harvest proceeds before releasing to farmer

Platform role

Loan servicer (not lender); NBFC carries credit risk; platform earns referral fee

AQUAOS

Aquaculture Ecosystem Platform



Product Requirements Document

Farm OS  ┬╖  Advisory Engine  ┬╖  Input Marketplace  ┬╖  Harvest Marketplace  ┬╖  Farmer Community

Primary Launch Geography: Andhra Pradesh, India

Architecture  ┬╖  Security  ┬╖  UI/UX  ┬╖  API  ┬╖  Database  ┬╖  DevOps  ┬╖  Monetization



1. Executive Summary

AquaOS is a mobile-first, serverless aquaculture operating system purpose-built for India's rapidly expanding shrimp and fish farming industry, with an initial focus on the high-density aquaculture districts of Andhra Pradesh. The platform eliminates the four chronic pain-points of Indian aquaculture: information asymmetry, input procurement inefficiency, harvest price opacity, and operational data blindness.



Mission: Empower every aquaculture farmer in India with enterprise-grade tools, fair market access, and intelligent advisory ΓÇö free of cost, forever.



1.1 The Opportunity

India is the second-largest shrimp producer globally, with Andhra Pradesh contributing over 70% of national aquaculture output. Yet 80% of farmers operate without any digital tools, rely on middlemen for both inputs and harvest sales, and receive no real-time market pricing data. The average farmer loses 22ΓÇô35% of potential income to inefficiency. AquaOS captures this value and distributes it back to farmers, while monetising the ecosystem through buyer subscriptions, supplier listings, and transaction commissions.



1.2 Core Value Propositions

Stakeholder

Core Value Delivered

Measurable Impact

Farmer

Free digital farm OS + market access + advisory

+18ΓÇô30% net income

Buyer

Direct verified harvest listings + real-time offers

ΓêÆ15% procurement cost

Supplier

Digital storefront + direct farmer leads

+35% conversion rate

Platform

Commission + subscription revenue + data asset

Γé╣200Cr+ GMV Year 3

Ecosystem

Transparent pricing + reduced food miles

Supply chain efficiency



1.3 Zero-Cost Infrastructure Model

The platform is architected on a 100% serverless, pay-per-use model. During pre-revenue phases, cloud costs approximate Γé╣0 for workloads under free-tier thresholds. As revenue scales, infrastructure scales proportionally with near-zero fixed overhead. This eliminates the burn-rate trap that kills agri-tech startups.

Cloud Provider

AWS (primary) with multi-cloud readiness

Compute

AWS Lambda ΓÇö 1M requests/month free forever

Database

PlanetScale (MySQL serverless) ΓÇö 5GB free tier

Storage

Cloudflare R2 ΓÇö 10GB free, zero egress cost

CDN

Cloudflare ΓÇö unlimited bandwidth free tier

Auth

Supabase Auth ΓÇö 50,000 MAU free

Email

Resend ΓÇö 3,000 emails/day free tier

SMS

MSG91 ΓÇö pay-per-SMS, no fixed cost

Monitoring

Sentry + UptimeRobot ΓÇö free tiers

CI/CD

4. Platform Architecture

4.1 High-Level System Architecture

AquaOS adopts a serverless microservices architecture designed for zero operational cost at launch, elastic scalability to 100,000+ concurrent users, and zero-downtime deployments. All components are managed services, eliminating server management overhead entirely.



Architecture Principle: No servers. No maintenance. Pay only for actual usage.



4.2 Technology Stack ΓÇö Complete Specification

Justification

Offline-first data persistence for intermittent connectivity

Dio + Retrofit

Type-safe API client with interceptors for auth + retry

4.3 Microservices Architecture

Each service is an independent Lambda function group with its own database namespace, deployed via separate CloudFormation stacks. Services communicate via REST internally (no inter-Lambda direct calls) through the API Gateway, ensuring loose coupling.



Service

Responsibility

Key Tables

External Dependencies

User Service

Auth, profiles, roles, KYC

Crop cycles, feed logs, mortality

Open-Meteo Weather API

4.4 Database Architecture

Primary Schema ΓÇö Core Tables

All tables use UUID primary keys for global uniqueness, include created_at/updated_at timestamps, and implement soft deletes via deleted_at for data recovery. PlanetScale's branching feature enables zero-downtime schema migrations.

5. Module Specifications

5.1 Farm OS Module

The Farm OS is the core operational layer of AquaOS. It digitises every aspect of aquaculture farm management, creating a comprehensive operational record that powers advisory, marketplace trust, and analytics. The Farm OS is the primary daily engagement driver and the data moat of the platform.



5.1.1 Farm Creation & Management

Screen

Farm Setup Wizard (3-step onboarding)

Step 1

Farm name, district, mandal, village

Step 2

Total acres, water source, GPS (auto or manual)

Step 3

Privacy settings (location visibility)

API

POST /v1/farms ΓÇö returns farm ID + dashboard activation

Validation

District must be valid AP district; area between 0.1ΓÇô5000 acres

Offline

Form data cached locally, synced on connectivity restore



5.1.2 Pond Management

Screen

Pond Manager (grid view + individual pond cards)

Pond Card Shows

Name, area, current crop species, crop age (days), health status

Add Pond Flow

Name ΓåÆ area ΓåÆ depth ΓåÆ aerator count ΓåÆ shape ΓåÆ save

Visual

Color-coded status: Green (healthy), Amber (advisory alert), Red (critical)

API

POST /v1/farms/{id}/ponds, GET /v1/farms/{id}/ponds

Max Ponds

100 per farm (soft limit, configurable by admin)



5.1.3 Crop Cycle Management

A crop cycle is the fundamental operational unit in AquaOS. Every advisory, feed log, mortality log, and harvest listing is anchored to a crop cycle. The system supports parallel cycles across multiple ponds.

Start Crop Flow

Species ΓåÆ stocking date ΓåÆ seed count ΓåÆ seed source ΓåÆ target size

Auto-Calculations

Seed density (count/sqm), expected harvest date, crop age (days)

Dashboard Triggers

Crop creation activates: daily feed reminders, advisory monitoring, survival tracking

Supported Species

Whiteleg Shrimp (L. vannamei), Tiger Prawn, Rohu, Catla, Crab, Others

API

POST /v1/ponds/{id}/crops, GET /v1/ponds/{id}/crops/active



5.1.4 Feed Logging

Screen

Feed Log Entry (daily tap-and-go interface)

Fields

Feed brand (autocomplete from product catalog), quantity (kg), stage (morning/afternoon/evening)

Smart Defaults

Last-used brand pre-filled; quantity suggestion based on FCR model

Reminder

Daily push notification at 7 AM if feed not logged

Running Total

Cumulative feed displayed on pond card (auto-updates FCR)

API

POST /v1/crops/{id}/feed-logs, GET /v1/crops/{id}/feed-logs



5.1.5 Mortality Logging

Screen

Mortality Log Entry (quick-entry with symptom checklist)

Fields

Date, count, observed symptoms (multi-select), notes

Symptom Library

20+ pre-defined symptoms (white spot, black gill, EMS, etc.)

Alert Trigger

Mortality > 0.5% of stock/day ΓåÆ immediate advisory alert

Running Total

Updates estimated survival rate on crop dashboard

API

POST /v1/crops/{id}/mortality-logs



5.1.6 Water Quality Logging

Parameters

DO (mg/L), pH, salinity (ppt), temperature (┬░C), turbidity, ammonia

Entry Method

Manual entry or future IoT sensor auto-sync

Alert Thresholds

DO < 4 mg/L ΓåÆ critical alert; pH < 7 or > 8.5 ΓåÆ warning

API

POST /v1/crops/{id}/water-logs



5.1.7 Farm Dashboard

The Farm Dashboard is the home screen for farmers ΓÇö a single-screen operational overview of all active ponds and crops. It is designed for sub-3-second load even on 2G networks through aggressive server-side caching.

Active Ponds

Card-based layout, one card per active crop

Each Card Shows

Pond name, species, crop age, estimated survival %, FCR, feed today (Y/N)

Summary Strip

Total active ponds | Total estimated harvest (kg) | Advisory alerts count

Quick Actions

Log Feed, Log Mortality, View Advisory, List Harvest

Refresh

Pull-to-refresh; auto-refresh every 10 min in background

Cache Strategy

Dashboard response cached in Redis 5 min TTL, invalidated on any write

API

GET /v1/farmer/dashboard ΓÇö aggregated response, single API call



5.2 Advisory Engine

The Advisory Engine is the intelligence layer of AquaOS. It processes farm operational data, weather inputs, and a curated rule library to generate actionable, farmer-friendly alerts in Telugu and English. The engine runs as a scheduled Lambda function, evaluating all active crops every 6 hours.



5.2.1 Advisory Input Sources

Crop Data

Age, species, stocking density, FCR, mortality rate (from Crop Service)

Feed Data

Daily consumption vs. target, missed feed days (from Feed Logs)

Water Quality

DO, pH, salinity, temperature (from Water Logs or IoT)

Weather Data

Open-Meteo API: temperature, rainfall, wind speed, humidity forecast

Disease Reports

Community-reported outbreaks in same district (from Community Service)

Mortality Patterns

Mortality rate acceleration over 72-hour rolling window



5.2.2 Advisory Rule Library

Rule Name

Trigger Condition

Severity

Recommended Action

Feed Overuse Alert

FCR > 2.2 for 3 consecutive days

MEDIUM

Reduce feed by 20%, check DO levels

Missed Feed Alert

No feed log for 24h on active crop

LOW

Log feed; check pond accessibility

Mortality Spike

Mortality > 0.5% stock/24h

HIGH

Collect samples, contact advisor

Low DO Warning

DO < 4.5 mg/L logged

HIGH

Increase aeration immediately

pH Imbalance

pH < 7.0 or > 8.8

MEDIUM

Apply lime or organic acid

Disease Nearby

Outbreak reported within 30km

MEDIUM

Increase monitoring frequency

Storm Warning

Rain > 50mm/24h forecast

HIGH

Reduce feed, check pond bunds

Heat Stress

Water temp > 33┬░C logged or forecast

MEDIUM

Reduce feeding, increase aeration

Harvest Window

Crop age > 90 days + size estimate meets target

INFO

Consider harvest, check market prices

Low Survival Projection

Estimated survival < 60%

HIGH

Emergency advisor consultation

FCR Improving

FCR decreasing for 5 days

INFO

Good performance ΓÇö maintain regime

Weight Milestone

Estimated size crosses key count threshold

INFO

Sample size, update listing estimate



5.2.3 Advisory Delivery

Push Notification

Immediate delivery for HIGH severity alerts

In-App Banner

Persistent until acknowledged for MEDIUM+ alerts

SMS Fallback

Sent if push notification not delivered within 10 min (HIGH only)

Language

Telugu default; English optional; Hindi future

Advisory History

All alerts stored 12 months; accessible from farm dashboard

Snooze

Farmer can snooze MEDIUM alerts 24h; HIGH alerts cannot be snoozed



5.3 Harvest Marketplace

The Harvest Marketplace is a verified, real-time exchange connecting farmers to buyers. All listings are anchored to actual farm data, making them more credible than traditional broker-mediated transactions.



5.3.1 Harvest Listing Creation

Screen

Create Listing (4 fields, 2 taps to publish)

Species

Auto-populated from active crop (editable)

Size/Count

Count per kg (e.g. 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80) ΓÇö dropdown

Quantity

Kg available (minimum 50 kg)

Harvest Date

Calendar picker (today or future, max 14 days)

Asking Price

Optional; market price shown as reference

Photos

Upload up to 5 pond/shrimp photos (compressed to 800px, stored in R2)

Urgent Flag

Toggle ΓÇö surfaces listing higher in buyer search

Expiry

Auto-expires in 72 hours; farmer notified 12h before expiry

District

Auto-populated from farm; visible to buyers

API

POST /v1/harvest-listings ΓÇö returns listing ID + WhatsApp share link



5.3.2 Buyer Discovery & Search

Search Filters

Species, district, size count range, harvest date range, quantity

Sort Options

Newest, harvest date, quantity (high/low), price (if provided)

Listing Card

Species, size, quantity, location (district), harvest date, asking price (if set)

Map View

District-level clusters (not exact farm location unless farmer opted in)

Saved Searches

Buyers save filter presets; notified when matching listings appear

Verified Badge

Listings from verified farmers (completed 2+ crop cycles) get a badge

API

GET /v1/harvest-listings?species=&district=&date_from=&date_to=&size_min=&size_max=



5.3.3 Offer & Negotiation System

Offer Submission

Buyer enters price per kg + quantity + notes ΓåÆ sends to farmer

Farmer Actions

Accept, Reject, Counter-offer

Counter Flow

Farmer enters counter-price ΓåÆ buyer gets notification ΓåÆ same flow

Chat Integration

Each offer automatically opens a conversation thread in Chat Service

Offer Expiry

Buyer offer expires in 24 hours if no farmer response

Status Tracking

Both parties see offer status in real-time via Supabase Realtime

Transaction Record

Accepted offer logged as transaction in Payment Service

Commission

Platform charges 1.5% of transaction value (collected from buyer)

API

POST /v1/harvest-listings/{id}/offers, PATCH /v1/offers/{id}



5.4 Input Marketplace

The Input Marketplace creates a competitive, transparent procurement environment for aquaculture inputs. Farmers compare products, prices, and supplier ratings ΓÇö breaking the local dealer monopoly.



5.4.1 Product Categories

Feed

Branded pellet feed by stage (starter, grower, finisher) + species

Seed / Post-larvae

Certified PLs from registered hatcheries

Medicines & Chemicals

Antibiotics, probiotics, disinfectants (licensed suppliers only)

Probiotics

Water and gut probiotics by brand

Aerators

Paddle wheel, propeller types by horsepower

Equipment

DO meters, pH meters, nets, sampling equipment

Pond Prep Chemicals

Lime, dolomite, fertilizers



5.4.2 Supplier Onboarding

Registration

Business name, GSTIN, license type, district coverage

Verification

Admin manually verifies GSTIN and license within 48 hours

Product Listing

Supplier adds products with: name, brand, category, unit, MRP, offer price, minimum order

Subscription

Γé╣6,000ΓÇôΓé╣15,000/month depending on listing count and featured placement

Analytics Access

Supplier sees: views, inquiries, conversion rates, district demand heatmap

API

POST /v1/suppliers, POST /v1/products, GET /v1/products?category=&district=



5.4.3 Farmer Procurement Flow

Discovery

Browse by category ΓåÆ filter by brand, district, price ΓåÆ product detail

Inquiry

Tap 'Get Quote' ΓåÆ fills quantity ΓåÆ message sent to supplier

Lead Routing

Supplier receives push notification + email with farmer contact

Order Tracking

Basic order status (confirmed, dispatched, delivered) via manual supplier update

Reviews

Post-delivery, farmer rates supplier 1ΓÇô5 stars with optional comment

Price Intelligence

MRP displayed; offer price shown; historical lowest price shown



5.5 Farmer Community

The Farmer Community is a Telugu-first knowledge network where farmers share experiences, ask questions, and build peer trust. It reduces reliance on unverified WhatsApp forwards and creates a verified, moderated knowledge base.



Post Types

Text post, photo post, question post, poll

Tags

Species, topic (feed, disease, harvest, general)

Language

Telugu primary, Hindi, English supported

Image Upload

Up to 4 images per post (compressed, R2 stored)

Engagement

Like, comment, share (WhatsApp share), save post

Verification Badge

Farmers with 3+ completed crop cycles get 'Experienced' badge

Advisor Badge

Platform-verified advisors get 'Expert' badge

Trending

Algorithm based on engagement rate within 48h

Moderation

Admin flags + community reports ΓåÆ admin review queue

Abuse Detection

Keyword filter + report system; 3 reports ΓåÆ auto-suspend pending review

API

POST /v1/posts, GET /v1/posts?tag=&sort=trending, POST /v1/posts/{id}/comments



6. Complete API Specification

6.1 API Design Principles

Base URL

https://api.aquaos.in/v1

Protocol

HTTPS only (TLS 1.3 minimum)

Format

JSON request and response bodies

Authentication

Bearer JWT in Authorization header for all private endpoints

Versioning

URL path versioning (/v1, /v2); old versions supported 12 months

Rate Limiting

100 req/min per user (public), 500 req/min (buyer/supplier)

Pagination

Cursor-based for lists: ?cursor=&limit=20 (max 100)

Error Format

{ error: { code, message, field?, details? } }

HTTP Status

200 OK, 201 Created, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauth, 403 Forbidden, 404, 429, 500

Idempotency

POST requests accept Idempotency-Key header for safe retries

7. UI/UX Design System

7.1 Design Philosophy

AquaOS's interface is built for the semi-urban farmer in Andhra Pradesh. The design language prioritises functional clarity over aesthetic complexity. Every screen must work comfortably on a Γé╣7,000 Android smartphone with a cracked screen in bright sunlight. The UI is Telugu-first, data-light, and built for one-handed use.



Principle

Implementation

Telugu First

All UI strings in Telugu by default; English toggle in settings

Large Touch Targets

Minimum 48├ù48dp tap targets; primary actions ΓëÑ 56dp height

High Contrast

WCAG AA compliant contrast ratios for outdoor readability

Offline-First

All critical screens load from local SQLite cache when offline

Progressive Disclosure

Show only essential info; detail on tap ΓÇö no cognitive overload

Skeleton Loading

All data loads show skeleton UI ΓÇö never blank white screens

Error Recovery

Every error state has a clear retry action and human-readable message in Telugu

Minimal Input

Smart defaults, autocomplete, toggles over free text wherever possible

Data Light

All API responses optimised for < 10KB; images lazy loaded

Icon + title (Telugu) + description + recommended action

Action Button

Primary CTA specific to alert type (e.g. 'Reduce Feed' ΓåÆ opens feed log)

Acknowledge

Swipe right or 'Got It' button

Button to connect with platform advisor for HIGH severity

History

Separate screen: all alerts sorted by date, filterable by pond



8. Security Architecture

AquaOS handles sensitive farmer financial data, farm location data, and transaction records. Security is non-negotiable and is designed into every layer of the platform ΓÇö not added as an afterthought.



Security Standard: OWASP Top 10 compliance + India DPDP Act 2023 compliance



8.1 Authentication & Authorization

Authentication Method

OTP-based (phone number) ΓÇö no passwords to steal or forget

OTP Provider

MSG91 with 6-digit OTP, 5-minute expiry

OTP Rate Limiting

Max 3 OTPs per phone per 15 minutes; 10 per day

Token Type

JWT (RS256 signed) ΓÇö asymmetric signing, public key verification

Access Token TTL

15 minutes ΓÇö short-lived to limit exposure window

Refresh Token TTL

30 days ΓÇö rotated on each use (refresh token rotation)

Refresh Token Storage

Stored in device secure storage (Flutter Secure Storage)

Token Blacklist

Upstash Redis stores invalidated JTIs for immediate logout

Role System

RBAC: farmer, buyer, supplier, advisor, admin ΓÇö enforced at API Gateway

Row-Level Security

Database queries include user_id filter; no trust in client-side IDs



8.2 API Security

Transport

TLS 1.3 minimum; HSTS enforced; HTTP ΓåÆ HTTPS redirect

Rate Limiting

Per-user: 100 req/min; Per-IP: 500 req/min; Login: 5 attempts/15min

API Gateway WAF

AWS WAF with OWASP Core Rule Set ΓÇö blocks SQLi, XSS, RCE patterns

CORS

Strict allowlist: app://aquaos, https://admin.aquaos.in only

Input Validation

All inputs validated with Zod schemas before processing

SQL Injection

Parameterised queries only (Drizzle ORM); no raw SQL with user input

Request Signing

Mobile app signs critical requests with device keypair (Phase 2)

Fraud Detection

Unusual patterns (bulk offer submissions, fake listings) flagged and reviewed

Content Security

File uploads: MIME type validation + virus scan (ClamAV Lambda layer)



8.3 Data Privacy (DPDP Act 2023 Compliance)

Data Minimisation

Collect only fields required for stated purpose

Consent Recording

Explicit consent captured at registration; stored with timestamp

Location Privacy

Farm GPS stored encrypted; not shared without farmer consent

Data Subject Rights

In-app: Download My Data + Delete My Account (30-day deletion)

Third-Party Sharing

Only with buyer/supplier as part of transaction; disclosed in ToS

Data Retention

Active: indefinite; Deleted accounts: 30-day soft delete then purge

DPO

Data Protection Officer role defined; contact in app settings

Breach Response

72-hour notification to CERT-In and affected users per DPDP Act



8.4 Infrastructure Security

Secrets Management

AWS Secrets Manager ΓÇö no secrets in environment variables or code

VPC Isolation

Lambda functions in private VPC; database not publicly accessible

Database Access

Only Lambda VPC security group allowed; no direct internet access

Encryption at Rest

PlanetScale: encrypted at rest (AES-256); R2: SSE enabled

Encryption in Transit

TLS 1.3 for all service-to-service communication

IAM Least Privilege

Each Lambda has minimal IAM permissions; no admin roles

Dependency Scanning

GitHub Dependabot scans all dependencies; critical CVEs ΓåÆ immediate patch

Security Headers

CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options on all responses

Audit Logging

All admin actions logged to append-only Axiom log stream



8.5 Farmer Data Privacy Controls

Farmers have granular control over what data is visible on the platform. Privacy controls are available in Profile Settings:

Farm Location

Toggle: Show district only | Show village | Show GPS (default: district only)

Production Volume

Toggle: Hide total harvest volume from public (default: hidden)

Contact Details

Toggle: Allow buyers to see phone after offer accepted (default: after acceptance)

Community Identity

Toggle: Show farm name in community | Anonymous posts

Data Analytics

Toggle: Contribute anonymous farm data to industry analytics

Cache Layer

TTL

9.3 Scalability Design

The serverless architecture inherently scales horizontally without any configuration. The following design decisions ensure the system handles 100,000 concurrent farmers without architectural changes:

Compute Scaling

AWS Lambda: concurrent executions scale from 0 to 10,000 automatically

Database Scaling

PlanetScale: auto-scales read replicas; sharding ready for 1B+ rows

Search Scaling

Meilisearch: horizontal scaling via multiple instances on Fly.io

CDN Scaling

Cloudflare: global edge network handles any traffic volume

Chat Scaling

Supabase Realtime: 200 concurrent on free; upgrade to Pro at 10K MAU for $25/month

Storage Scaling

Cloudflare R2: unlimited storage, no egress cost ΓÇö scales infinitely

Notification Scaling

FCM: Google's infrastructure handles millions/second natively

Database Connection Pooling

PlanetScale serverless driver ΓÇö no connection pool exhaustion



9.4 Offline Architecture

Network connectivity in rural Andhra Pradesh is intermittent. AquaOS must work offline for core farm management functions.

Feature

Offline Capability

Sync Strategy

Farm Dashboard

Full offline from local SQLite cache

Background sync on connectivity restore

Feed Logging

Full offline ΓÇö stores locally

Queued sync with conflict resolution (last-write-wins)

Mortality Logging

Full offline ΓÇö stores locally

Queued sync on connectivity restore

Water Quality Logging

Full offline ΓÇö stores locally

Queued sync on connectivity restore

Advisory Alerts

Shows last-received alerts from cache

New alerts delivered when online

Market Prices

Last 24h prices cached locally

Refresh on connectivity

Harvest Listings

View only from cache; create requires connectivity

Sync when online

Community Posts

View cached posts; post creation requires connectivity

Draft saved locally

Chat

Offline reads; sends queued

Queue processed on connectivity



10. DevOps, Infrastructure & Deployment

10.1 Infrastructure-as-Code

All cloud infrastructure is defined in AWS CDK (TypeScript). This ensures reproducible environments, version-controlled infrastructure, and zero manual console configuration. A single command deploys the entire platform.



IaC Tool

AWS CDK v2 (TypeScript)

Environments

dev, staging, production ΓÇö identical stacks, different configs

Config Management

AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store (non-secret configs)

Secrets

AWS Secrets Manager (API keys, DB credentials, JWT signing keys)

Deployment Command

cdk deploy --all (deploys all stacks in dependency order)

Stack Separation

VPC Stack ΓåÆ Auth Stack ΓåÆ Database Stack ΓåÆ API Stack ΓåÆ Frontend Stack



10.2 CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)

Stage

Trigger

Steps

Time

PR Check

Pull request opened/updated

Lint + TypeScript check + Unit tests + Security scan

< 3 min

Staging Deploy

Merge to develop branch

Build + integration tests + deploy to staging + smoke tests

< 8 min

Production Deploy

Tag release (v*.*.*)

Build + E2E tests + CDK deploy + Slack notification

< 12 min

Mobile Build

Tag release or manual trigger

Flutter build APK + upload to Play Store (internal track)

< 15 min

Rollback

Manual trigger or automatic on smoke test fail

CDK deploy previous version

< 5 min



10.3 Monitoring & Alerting

Uptime Monitoring

UptimeRobot ΓÇö checks all API endpoints every 1 minute; PagerDuty alert on failure

Error Tracking

Sentry ΓÇö captures Lambda exceptions; groups by error fingerprint

Application Logs

Axiom ΓÇö structured JSON logs from all Lambda functions; 7-day free retention

Performance Monitoring

AWS X-Ray ΓÇö traces request flow across Lambda + database

Business Metrics

Mixpanel ΓÇö farmer DAU, listing creation rate, offer acceptance rate

Database Monitoring

PlanetScale built-in query insights; slow query alerts

Cost Monitoring

AWS Cost Explorer + budget alerts at $10, $50, $100 thresholds

Alerting Channel

Slack #ops-alerts for warnings; PagerDuty for critical (downtime)

11. Monetisation Strategy

AquaOS's monetisation model is designed so that farmers are always free ΓÇö their trust is the platform's most valuable asset. All revenue is generated from the commercial participants (buyers and suppliers) who benefit from access to the farmer network.



11.1 Revenue Streams

Stream

Who Pays

Model

Price

Y1 Target

Buyer Premium Subscription

Exporters, processors

Monthly SaaS

Γé╣8,000ΓÇô25,000/month

50 buyers = Γé╣60L/year

Supplier Listing Subscription

Feed, medicine suppliers

Monthly SaaS

Γé╣3,000ΓÇô15,000/month

100 suppliers = Γé╣72L/year

Harvest Transaction Commission

Buyer

% of transaction

1.5% of harvest GMV

Γé╣200Cr GMV = Γé╣3Cr

Input Transaction Commission

Supplier

% of order value

2% of order value

Γé╣50Cr GMV = Γé╣1Cr

Γé╣2,000ΓÇô8,000/week

Γé╣30L/year

12.2 Notification Preferences

Users have granular control over notification preferences. The default state is optimised for engagement without overwhelming. Farmers can customise per-channel and per-type settings from Profile ΓåÆ Notification Settings.

Feed Reminder

ON by default ΓÇö daily 7 AM if feed not logged

Advisory Alerts

ON by default ΓÇö cannot fully disable HIGH severity

Market Prices

OFF by default ΓÇö farmer opts in

Community Replies

ON by default ΓÇö can disable

Buyer Offers

ON by default ΓÇö cannot disable (critical)

Supplier Promotions

OFF by default ΓÇö farmer opts in

Quiet Hours

Configurable: default 10 PM ΓÇô 6 AM (no non-critical notifications)



13. Admin Panel Specification

13.1 Admin Dashboard Overview

The Admin Panel is a Next.js web application accessible only from pre-authorised IP addresses with mandatory 2FA. It is the operational control center for AquaOS.



13.2 Admin Module Breakdown

User Management

View

All users with filters: role, district, registration date, verification status

Actions

Verify farmer, verify supplier, suspend user, impersonate (read-only) for support

KYC Queue

Unverified suppliers sorted by registration date; approve/reject with note

Fraud Flags

Users flagged by system (fake listings, bulk operations) for review



Harvest Listings Monitoring

View

All active listings with status, district, species, price deviation from market

Actions

Remove listing (with reason notification to farmer), flag for review

Price Integrity

Listings with price > 30% above/below market auto-flagged for review



Supplier Verification

Queue

New supplier registrations with GSTIN, license upload, business proof

Verification

Admin verifies GSTIN via GST portal API + license authenticity

Outcome

Approve (live in marketplace) / Reject (with reason) / Request more info



Community Moderation

Report Queue

Posts/comments reported by community, sorted by report count

Actions

Remove post, warn user, suspend user (7 days / 30 days / permanent)

Auto-Flagged

Posts with 3+ reports auto-hidden pending review

Keyword Filter

Configurable blocked keyword list (scam, competitor brand attacks, etc.)



Platform Analytics Dashboard

Farmer Metrics

Daily registrations, MAU, crop cycles started, feed logs, dashboard opens

Marketplace Metrics

Listings created, offers submitted, acceptance rate, GMV by day/district

Supplier Metrics

Inquiries sent, conversion rate, top products by views

Advisory Metrics

Alerts generated, acknowledgement rate, alert types distribution

Revenue Metrics

Active subscriptions, commission collected, MRR growth

Geographic Heatmap

Farmer density + activity by district on AP map



Advisory Rule Management

Rule Editor

No-code rule builder: IF [metric] [operator] [value] THEN [alert type] [message]

A/B Testing

Test two alert messages; track acknowledgement rate

Rule History

Audit log of all rule changes with author + timestamp

14.3 Regional Intelligence Reports

AquaOS's aggregated data creates a unique regional intelligence asset. The following reports will be generated monthly and can be licensed to industry bodies, NBFCs, and state departments:

District Supply Forecast

Upcoming harvest volumes by species, size, district ΓÇö 14-day forward view

Disease Outbreak Heatmap

Anonymous mortality spike clustering by district and symptom

Input Price Index

Average input prices by category across all listed suppliers

Feed Efficiency Benchmark

Average FCR by species, district, season ΓÇö anonymised

Survival Rate Trends

Survival rates over time ΓÇö useful for MPEDA and insurance providers

Harvest Price Index

Weighted average transaction prices by species/size/district



15. Go-to-Market & Launch Strategy

15.1 Pilot Launch Plan (Month 1ΓÇô3)

The pilot launch focuses exclusively on West Godavari district with 500 hand-selected farmers. This controlled environment allows rapid iteration before district-wide rollout. The pilot is designed to generate the first success stories that will fuel organic word-of-mouth growth.



Pilot District

Bhimavaram area, West Godavari

Pilot Farmer Count

500 farmers (hand-selected via aquaculture society partnerships)

Pilot Buyer Count

20 registered buyers (local exporters)

Duration

8 weeks intensive pilot

Success Criteria

70%+ farmers log at least 3 feed entries; 10+ harvest transactions completed

Support Model

2 on-ground 'AquaOS Champions' ΓÇö local farmers trained as ambassadors

Onboarding Mode

Group onboarding sessions at mandal-level aquaculture societies

Feedback Loop

Weekly WhatsApp group with pilot farmers; bi-weekly in-person visits

16.4 Technical Debt Prevention Rules

The following rules are enforced from Day 1 of development to prevent compounding technical debt:

TypeScript Strict Mode

tsconfig strict: true ΓÇö no implicit any, strict null checks

Test Coverage

Minimum 70% coverage on all service layers; 90% on payment flows

API Documentation

OpenAPI 3.0 spec auto-generated and kept in sync; no API without docs

Database Migrations

All schema changes via PlanetScale branches ΓÇö no direct schema edits

Code Review

No PR merged without 1 approval; Tech Lead reviews all schema changes

Dependency Policy

All new dependencies require Tech Lead sign-off; no abandoned packages

Performance Budget

API responses must include execution time header; fail CI if > 2000ms

Error Handling

All async functions wrapped in try/catch; no silent failures

Logging Standards

Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs; no console.log in production

Feature Flags

All new features behind flags (Growthbook free tier); safe rollout



17. Legal, Compliance & Risk Management

17.1 Regulatory Compliance

India DPDP Act 2023

Full compliance: consent capture, data minimisation, erasure rights, DPO appointed

IT Act 2000

Platform intermediary protections; grievance officer contact in app

FSSAI

Food safety compliance for any platform-facilitated food transactions

Marine Products (MPEDA)

Harvest listings do not facilitate uncertified exports; advisory only

GST Compliance

Platform commission invoices with GST; marketplace participants responsible for own GST

RBI Payment Regulations

Razorpay handles PCI-DSS compliance; platform does not store card data

Consumer Protection Act 2019

Dispute resolution mechanism built into platform; 30-day resolution SLA



17.2 Risk Register

Risk

Probability

Impact

Mitigation

Farmer churn ΓÇö low engagement

Medium

High

Daily reminders, offline-first, Telugu UI, Champions program

Fake harvest listings

Medium

High

Verified farmer badges, crop cycle linkage, admin monitoring

Supplier fraud (counterfeit products)

Low

High

GSTIN verification, license check, community reviews, report system

Data breach

Low

Very High

Encryption, WAF, minimal data collection, incident response plan

OTP fraud / SIM swap

Low

High

Device binding (Phase 2), suspicious login alerts

Payment dispute (harvest)

Medium

Medium

Escrow option (Phase 2), in-app chat evidence, dispute resolution

Regulatory change (aquaculture policy)

Low

Medium

Stay updated with MPEDA/state dept; modular advisory content

AWS cost spike (traffic spike)

Low

Medium

Budget alerts, Lambda concurrency limits, cost monitoring

App store rejection

Low

Medium

Follow Google Play policies strictly; review guidelines before release

Competitor cloning

Medium

Low

Network effects moat, data moat, farmer trust is hardest to clone



17.3 Terms of Service Key Points

Platform Role

Intermediary ΓÇö not a party to buyer-seller transactions

Farmer Data Ownership

Farmer owns their farm data; can export or delete at any time

Dispute Liability

Platform not liable for transaction disputes; facilitates resolution

Content Moderation

Platform may remove content violating community guidelines

Price Transparency

Platform does not fix prices; all prices are farmer/buyer negotiated

Commission Disclosure

Commission rates clearly disclosed before transaction completion



18. Future Technology Roadmap (Year 2ΓÇô3)

18.1 AI Advisory Layer

Disease Prediction

CNN model on pond images + water quality data ΓåÆ early disease detection 5ΓÇô7 days before visible symptoms

Feed Optimisation Engine

ML model trained on FCR data across 100K+ crop cycles ΓåÆ personalised daily feed recommendations

Yield Prediction

Regression model using stocking density, feed, survival rate, weather ΓåÆ harvest yield estimate ┬▒10% accuracy

Price Prediction

Time-series model on 2+ years of transaction data ΓåÆ next-week price forecast per species/size/district

Technology

Python (scikit-learn, TensorFlow Lite), served via AWS Lambda inference endpoints

Data Requirement

Minimum 50,000 complete crop cycles for meaningful model training (~Year 2)



18.2 IoT Integration

Target Sensors

DO meter, pH meter, temperature sensor, salinity sensor ΓÇö integrated via MQTT

Protocol

MQTT over AWS IoT Core ΓÇö devices publish every 15 minutes

Data Ingestion

AWS IoT ΓåÆ Lambda ΓåÆ time-series storage in TimescaleDB or InfluxDB

Auto-Advisory

Out-of-range sensor readings trigger immediate advisory alerts without farmer manual entry

Dashboard Integration

Real-time sensor readings displayed on pond card with trend sparkline

Sensor Partners

Integration with YSI, Hanna Instruments, local APEDA-certified sensor manufacturers

Cost Model

Sensor hardware subsidised by platform (Γé╣2,000ΓÇô5,000/pond); subscription offsets



18.3 Financial Services Integration

Crop Loan Facilitation

Farm data as creditworthiness signal ΓåÆ NBFC partnerships for pre-approved crop loans

Crop Insurance

Automatic insurance quote based on crop data + weather risk ΓåÆ single-tap insurance purchase

Input Credit

Buy-now-pay-at-harvest model for feed purchases ΓåÆ solved with NBFC partner

Harvest Advance

Confirmed harvest listing ΓåÆ advance payment from buyer (5ΓÇô7 days before harvest)

Savings Product

Partner bank savings account with aquaculture-season-aware sweep features

19. Appendix

19.1 Glossary

Term

Definition

FCR

Feed Conversion Ratio ΓÇö kg of feed used per kg of shrimp/fish produced. Lower is better. Target < 1.8 for vannamei.

PL

Post-Larvae ΓÇö juvenile shrimp ready for stocking, counted per thousand

DO

Dissolved Oxygen ΓÇö critical water quality parameter; optimal 5ΓÇô8 mg/L for shrimp

Vannamei

Litopenaeus vannamei ΓÇö Pacific Whiteleg Shrimp; 80%+ of AP production

Count

Shrimp size expressed as number per kilogram (e.g. 30-count = 30 shrimp per kg)

GMV

Gross Merchandise Value ΓÇö total value of transactions through platform

MAU

Monthly Active Users

MPEDA

Marine Products Export Development Authority ΓÇö central govt seafood regulator

DPDP Act

Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 ΓÇö India's primary data privacy law

CDK

AWS Cloud Development Kit ΓÇö IaC tool for defining AWS infrastructure in TypeScript

JWT

JSON Web Token ΓÇö stateless authentication token

RBAC

Role-Based Access Control ΓÇö permission system based on user roles

MRR

Monthly Recurring Revenue ΓÇö subscription revenue earned each month

NPS

Net Promoter Score ΓÇö measure of user satisfaction and likelihood to recommend

WAF

Web Application Firewall ΓÇö security layer filtering malicious HTTP traffic

TTL

Time-to-Live ΓÇö duration for which cached data is considered valid

KISAN CONNECT

Rural Super-App





PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT

Version 1.0  |  Confidential & Proprietary



Document Type

Full Product Requirements Document

Status

Ready for Development

Platform

ΓÜá  STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL  ΓÜá

This document contains proprietary information. Unauthorized distribution is strictly prohibited.



Table of Contents







1. Executive Summary

Kisan Connect is a unified rural super-app designed to digitize and democratize the rural economy of India and eventually emerging markets across Asia and Africa. The platform integrates four high-frequency, high-trust marketplaces ΓÇö Crop Trading, Equipment Rental, Rural Services, and Rural Employment ΓÇö into a single, dual-mode application that serves both Buyers/Service Seekers and Sellers/Service Providers.



This Product Requirements Document (PRD) is the single authoritative source of truth for the design, engineering, and go-to-market execution of Kisan Connect. It is written to be handed directly to a cross-functional developer team and AI pair-programming assistants as a complete, actionable blueprint requiring zero follow-up documents.



Vision Statement

To become the indispensable digital infrastructure for 500 million rural citizens, enabling every farmer, artisan, tradesperson, and household to participate fully and fairly in the digital economy ΓÇö regardless of connectivity, device, or digital literacy level.



1.1 The Opportunity

India's rural economy accounts for nearly 46% of national income yet remains severely underserved by digital platforms. Farmers lose up to 30% of post-harvest value due to information asymmetry and middlemen. Equipment idle time costs billions annually. Service providers lack discovery mechanisms. Rural youth face structural unemployment despite growing skill levels. Kisan Connect addresses all four pain points through a single platform with a unified identity layer, shared payment rails, and a trust engine powered by escrow, reviews, and AI verification.



1.2 Core Differentiators

Offline-First Architecture: Full functionality in 2G/no-connectivity environments via local SQLite caching and background sync queues

Escrow-as-Standard: Every transaction ΓÇö crop, equipment, service ΓÇö is protected by a mandatory escrow engine with a structured dispute resolution state machine

AI Price Intelligence: Proprietary LSTM-based crop price prediction model trained on government APIs, satellite imagery, weather feeds, and crowdsourced farmer data

Dual-Mode UX: A single app switches seamlessly between Buyer and Seller mode, reducing fragmentation and increasing platform stickiness

Zero-Infrastructure Cost Bootstrap: Designed to run on free-tier cloud infrastructure (Supabase, Railway, Vercel, Cloudflare Workers) during MVP phase with a clear migration path to production-grade cloud

Regional Language First: Full UI/UX in 12 Indian languages with voice-input and text-to-speech at every input point



1.3 Revenue Model Summary

Revenue Stream

Marketplace

Model

Indicative Rate

Transaction Commission

2ΓÇô15%

Γé╣199ΓÇôΓé╣499/listing

Crop / Equipment

Service fee on delivery

Γé╣25ΓÇôΓé╣150/delivery



2. Product Philosophy & Design Principles

2.1 The Dual-Mode Paradigm

The foundational design decision of Kisan Connect is the "One App, Two Modes" architecture. Rather than building four separate apps with four separate user acquisition funnels, the platform presents a single entry point. Upon first registration, users select a Primary Mode. However, the system is engineered to allow any user to hold both a Buyer Profile and a Seller Profile simultaneously, switchable from a persistent toggle in the top navigation bar.



Design Rationale

A farmer who sells crops is also likely to rent equipment and hire labor. A service provider is also a job seeker. Forcing users into single-role apps contradicts the reality of rural economic behavior. The dual-mode approach recognizes and serves this reality, increasing engagement depth and platform lifetime value per user.



2.2 The Eight Design Laws

Every design decision ΓÇö from screen layout to API response structure ΓÇö must pass through these eight laws:



Law 1: Connectivity Agnosticism: The app must function fully on zero connectivity. Network is a bonus, not a requirement. Every feature must have an offline fallback.

Law 2: Literacy Inclusivity: The platform must be usable by a person who cannot read. Voice input, icon-first navigation, and audio feedback are not enhancements ΓÇö they are baseline requirements.

Law 3: Trust by Default: Every transaction carries escrow protection. Every user profile shows verification status. Every review is from a verified transaction. Trust is not earned over time on this platform ΓÇö it is structurally enforced from day one.

Law 4: Financial Inclusion: Zero minimum transaction value. UPI as the primary payment rail. No credit card required anywhere. Support for feature phone users via USSD fallback.

Law 5: Data Sovereignty: User data belongs to the user. Data is stored encrypted at rest and in transit. PDPB (India Personal Data Protection Bill) compliance is non-negotiable from day one, not retrofitted.

Law 6: Performance Parity: A user on a 2-year-old Γé╣5,000 Android device must have an experience comparable to a user on a flagship. APK size < 20MB. Initial load < 2 seconds on 3G. Every list screen must render first 10 items in < 500ms.

Law 7: Extreme Localization: Not just translation. Localization includes date formats, weight units (maund, quintal vs kg), currency display, cultural imagery, and local festival calendars in the AI price model.

Law 8: Reversible Architecture: Every decision must be reversible without a full rewrite. Microservices are isolated. Databases are per-service. Payment gateways are swapped behind an abstraction layer. This protects the billion-dollar codebase from vendor lock-in.



3. System Architecture

3.1 Architectural Overview

Kisan Connect is built on a microservices architecture orchestrated behind an API Gateway. Each bounded domain (User Management, each Marketplace, Payments, Notifications, AI/ML) is an independently deployable service with its own private database. Services communicate via asynchronous event queues (Kafka/RabbitMQ) for non-blocking operations and synchronous REST/gRPC calls for real-time queries.

3.2 Microservice Boundaries & Responsibilities

3.2.1 API Gateway

The API Gateway is the single ingress point for all client traffic. It handles:

JWT validation and token refresh before forwarding requests to downstream services

Rate limiting per user per endpoint (configurable per plan tier)

Request/response transformation for versioned APIs

SSL termination and DDoS protection via Cloudflare

Logging of every request with correlation IDs for distributed tracing

A/B testing header injection for experiment routing



3.2.2 User Management Service

Owns the users, user_locations, seller_profiles, buyer_profiles, and verification_documents tables

Issues signed JWTs (RS256) with role claims (buyer, seller, admin, support)

Handles OTP-based phone number authentication via SMS (primary) and WhatsApp (fallback)

Manages KYC document upload, storage, and status tracking

Exposes a gRPC endpoint for other services to validate tokens without round-tripping to the gateway



3.2.3 Marketplace Services (Crop / Equipment / Services / Jobs)

Each service owns its own PostgreSQL schema on Supabase

Communicates with User Service via gRPC for seller/buyer profile lookups

Publishes domain events (ListingCreated, OrderPlaced, BookingConfirmed) to the Kafka event bus

Maintains its own search index via webhook pushes to the Search Service on create/update/delete

Implements pagination using cursor-based pagination (not offset) for consistent results on real-time data



3.2.4 Unified Payment & Financial Services Cluster

The payment cluster is the most critical system and is designed for maximum fault tolerance. It consists of four independent services:

Payment Gateway Service: An abstraction layer over Razorpay (primary) and PhonePe (UPI-specific). Exposes a unified POST /v1/payments/initiate endpoint. Normalizes callback payloads across gateways. Enables hot-swapping of gateways without code changes in upstream services.

Transaction Service: Maintains an immutable append-only ledger of every payment event. Never deletes or updates records ΓÇö only inserts new state events. Provides the canonical source of truth for payment status.

Escrow Service: Implements a formal state machine (see Section 7.5). Holds funds in a designated bank escrow account. Manages dispute windows, arbitration escalation, and automated releases.

Commission & Ledger Service: Calculates and records commission splits in real time when escrow releases. Manages seller payout schedules (daily/weekly). Maintains separate ledger accounts for the platform, each seller wallet, and the refund reserve.



3.3 Event-Driven Communication

All cross-service communication that does not require an immediate synchronous response is handled via the Kafka event bus. This ensures services are loosely coupled and the system remains operational even if a downstream service is temporarily unavailable.



Event Name

Producer

Consumers

Payload Key Fields

User Service

Notification, Analytics

user_id, phone, language

listing.created

3.4 Infrastructure-as-Code (Zero INR Bootstrap Strategy)

The following architecture enables a full production-quality deployment with zero infrastructure cost during the MVP and early growth phases. Each component is selected for its generous free tier and a clear upgrade path.



Zero-Cost Stack Summary

Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Realtime) ΓÇö Free: 500MB DB, 1GB storage, 50K MAU | Railway.app ΓÇö Free: $5 credit/month per service | Cloudflare Workers ΓÇö Free: 100K req/day | Upstash Redis & Kafka ΓÇö Free: 10K commands/day | Firebase FCM ΓÇö Free: unlimited push notifications | HuggingFace Spaces ΓÇö Free: ML model hosting | Vercel ΓÇö Free: unlimited deployments | Sentry ΓÇö Free: 5K errors/month | Grafana Cloud ΓÇö Free: 10K metrics.



Migration Triggers: Upgrade to paid tiers when any of the following thresholds are crossed:

Database storage exceeds 400MB on Supabase ΓåÆ migrate to dedicated Supabase Pro ($25/month)

MAU exceeds 40,000 ΓåÆ upgrade Supabase Auth tier

Railway service memory exceeds 512MB consistently ΓåÆ upgrade to Starter plan ($5/month/service)

Cloudflare Workers requests exceed 80K/day ΓåÆ upgrade to Workers Paid ($5/month, 10M req)



4. Complete Database Schema

4.1 Design Principles

UUID primary keys everywhere ΓÇö prevents enumeration attacks and enables distributed ID generation

Every table has created_at and updated_at timestamps with automatic triggers

Soft deletes using deleted_at nullable timestamp ΓÇö no hard deletes on business data

JSONB for flexible metadata ΓÇö never create a column for data that varies per record type

Row-Level Security (RLS) enabled on all Supabase tables ΓÇö no service can read another service's data without explicit policy

All financial amounts stored as BIGINT in the smallest currency unit (paisa for INR) ΓÇö never use FLOAT for money

5. Security Architecture

Security Philosophy

Security is not a layer added after development. It is a first-class citizen baked into the architecture, database schema, API design, and deployment pipeline from day zero. This section defines the complete threat model, control specifications, and implementation requirements.



5.1 Authentication & Authorization

5.1.1 Authentication Stack

Primary Auth: Phone number + OTP (6-digit, 10-minute expiry, max 3 attempts, 30-minute lockout after failure)

OTP Delivery: Primary: SMS via MSG91/Twilio. Fallback: WhatsApp Business API. Final fallback: Voice call IVR

Token Type: JWT signed with RS256 (asymmetric). Access token TTL: 15 minutes. Refresh token TTL: 30 days (stored in HttpOnly cookie on web, Secure Keychain on mobile)

Token Rotation: Silent refresh using refresh token before access token expiry. Refresh token rotated on every use (one-time use tokens)

Device Binding: JWT payload includes device_fingerprint hash. Token invalid if device changes (requires re-auth)

Biometric Auth: After initial OTP, subsequent logins may use Face ID / Touch ID via platform biometric APIs, with the JWT refresh handled transparently



5.1.2 Authorization Model ΓÇö Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

Role

Scope

Key Permissions

buyer

Own data only

Browse listings, place orders, view own orders, manage own profile, initiate disputes on own orders

seller

Own listings/orders

Create/edit own listings, view incoming orders, update order status, view earnings, manage bank account

employer

Own job posts

Create/edit job postings, view applicants for own jobs, contact applicants

job_seeker

Own applications

Browse jobs, apply, track own applications, update resume profile

support

Read-only most data

View user profiles, orders, disputes ΓÇö cannot modify financial records

admin

Full platform

All of the above plus user suspension, dispute arbitration, commission config, refunds, broadcast notifications

super_admin

Full platform + infra

All admin permissions plus payment gateway config, platform fee changes, data export



Row-Level Security (RLS) Policies ΓÇö All Supabase tables enforce RLS. Example policies:

RLS Policy Example

CREATE POLICY "users_can_read_own_data" ON users FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id); CREATE POLICY "sellers_manage_own_listings" ON crop_listings FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = seller_id); CREATE POLICY "buyers_view_active_listings" ON crop_listings FOR SELECT USING (status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL);



5.2 Data Security

5.2.1 Encryption Standards

Data Type

Encryption Standard

Implementation

Passwords

Argon2id

Memory: 64MB, Iterations: 3, Parallelism: 4

Data at Rest (DB)

AES-256-GCM

Supabase/PostgreSQL encrypted volumes

Data in Transit

TLS 1.3

Enforced at Cloudflare edge, HSTS headers, no TLS 1.0/1.1

PII Fields (Aadhaar)

AES-256 + application-level

Field-level encryption on sensitive KYC data before DB storage

Bank Account Numbers

AES-256 + tokenization

Only last 4 digits stored in DB; full number via Razorpay tokenization

API Keys / Secrets

HashiCorp Vault / Doppler

Never hardcoded; injected as environment variables at runtime

Backup Data

AES-256

Encrypted before upload to cold storage

Media Files (CDN)

Signed URLs

All private documents accessed via time-limited signed URLs (15 min TTL)



5.3 API Security

Input Validation: All API inputs validated with Zod/Joi schemas. Reject requests with unknown fields. Strict type checking on all financial amounts.

SQL Injection Prevention: Parameterized queries only. ORM (Prisma/Drizzle) used for all DB operations. Raw SQL only in migrations, never in application code.

Rate Limiting: Applied at Cloudflare edge and API Gateway. Auth endpoints: 5 requests/minute. General API: 100 requests/minute. Listing creation: 10/minute. Payment initiation: 3/minute.

CORS Policy: Strict origin allowlist. Mobile app bypasses CORS (native client). Admin dashboard: origin-restricted to admin subdomain only.

Content Security Policy: Strict CSP headers on all web interfaces. No inline scripts. No eval(). Nonce-based script loading.

File Upload Security: Server-side MIME type validation (magic bytes, not extension). Max file size: 10MB for images, 25MB for documents. ClamAV antivirus scan on all uploads before CDN storage.

Dependency Security: npm audit and Snyk integrated in CI/CD pipeline. No dependencies with high/critical vulnerabilities merged to main. Automated dependency updates via Dependabot.



5.4 Fraud Detection & Prevention

5.4.1 Real-Time Fraud Signals

Signal

Detection Method

Automated Action

Multiple accounts same phone

Phone number uniqueness + device fingerprint

Block registration, flag for review

Abnormal listing price

Statistical deviation from market price (┬▒3╧â)

Hold listing for admin review before publishing

Rapid bulk listing creation

Rate limiting + velocity check (>10 listings/hour)

Temporary account suspension + CAPTCHA

Payment amount mismatch

Server-side amount verification before gateway call

Reject payment, log security event

Geo velocity anomaly

Login location changes faster than physically possible

Force re-authentication + security alert to user

Fake review attempt

Reviewer must have completed transaction with reviewee

Review submission blocked if no verified transaction

Escrow manipulation

Status transitions validated against allowed state machine

Reject invalid transitions, alert security team

Collusive transactions

Graph analysis: same device, IP, or bank account on both buyer/seller

Flag for manual review, hold payouts



6. Performance Engineering

Performance Mandate

A user on a Γé╣5,000 Android device with 3G connectivity must perceive the app as fast. This is not a performance goal ΓÇö it is a product requirement. Users who experience slowness will not return. In rural India, switching costs are low and alternatives (WhatsApp groups, local agents) are always available.



6.1 Performance Budgets

6.2 Backend Performance Optimizations

6.2.1 Database Query Optimization

Mandatory indexes: All foreign keys, all columns used in WHERE clauses, all columns used in ORDER BY on paginated queries

PostGIS spatial indexes (GIST) on all geography columns for sub-50ms radius queries

Composite indexes for multi-column filter patterns: (status, created_at), (seller_id, status), (location geohash, status)

Cursor-based pagination on all list endpoints ΓÇö no OFFSET pagination on large tables

Query explain analyze run in CI for all new queries ΓÇö no sequential scans on tables > 10K rows allowed to merge

Materialized views for expensive aggregations (seller dashboard stats, category market prices) refreshed every 15 minutes

Connection pooling via PgBouncer (Supabase managed) ΓÇö max 25 connections per service, transaction-level pooling



6.2.2 Caching Strategy

Data Type

Cache Layer

TTL

Invalidation Strategy

Active listing data

Upstash Redis

5 minutes

Event-driven: invalidate on listing update/delete

User profile data

In-process memory (LRU)

2 minutes

Invalidate on profile update event

Category lists

Upstash Redis

24 hours

Manual flush on admin category change

Market price data

Upstash Redis

1 hour

Scheduled refresh from external APIs

Search results

Meilisearch internal cache

30 seconds

Auto-invalidated on index update

Crop price predictions

Upstash Redis

6 hours

ML model deployment triggers invalidation

Static app config

CDN Edge Cache

7 days

Cache-busting via content hash in URL

Seller stats (dashboard)

Upstash Redis

15 minutes

Scheduled recalculation via cron



6.3 Mobile Performance Optimizations

FlatList / LazyColumn with keyExtractor and getItemLayout for 60fps scrolling on long listing lists

React.memo / useMemo aggressive memoization on all list item components

Image lazy loading with progressive placeholders (blurHash low-res placeholder while full image loads)

Bundle splitting: marketplace modules loaded lazily on first navigation, reducing initial bundle size

Hermes JavaScript engine (React Native) ΓÇö reduces JS bundle parse time by ~60% on low-end devices

Proguard/R8 code shrinking on Android release builds ΓÇö reduces DEX size by ~30%

App startup: deferring all non-critical initializations (analytics, push registration) to after first screen render

WebP image format with AVIF fallback ΓÇö 30-50% smaller than JPEG at equivalent quality ΓÇö served by Cloudflare Image Resizing
