7.2 Component Library Specifications

Primary Button (Buyer CTA)

Height: 56dp. Corner radius: 12dp. Horizontal padding: 24dp. Full-width by default in forms.

Minimum tap target: 48dp ├ù 48dp (Material Design accessible minimum).

States: Default (primary bg, white text), Pressed (5% darker), Loading (spinner centered, text hidden), Disabled (40% opacity, no press feedback).

Haptic feedback: Medium impact on press. Confirmation vibration on success.



Listing Card (Marketplace)

16dp corner radius. 2dp elevation shadow. Aspect ratio of thumbnail image: 16:9.

Price always displayed in large bold text (text-headline) with accent color.

Verification badge (green check) displayed directly on seller avatar thumbnail if verified.

Distance badge (e.g., "12 km away") always shown in top-right corner of card.

Swipe-to-save gesture for wishlist on buyer mode cards.



7.3 Voice Input System

Voice input is not an optional feature ΓÇö it is a primary input method. Implementation requirements:

Voice input button (microphone icon) present on every text input field in the app

Language auto-detected from user's preferred_language setting, with manual override

Real-time transcription displayed as user speaks (streaming ASR via Google Speech-to-Text API)

Confirmation UI: User sees transcribed text before form submission, with easy tap-to-edit

Voice search: Holding the main search bar microphone icon activates voice search mode

Text-to-speech on key information screens: Price predictions, escrow status, payment confirmations are read aloud if TTS mode enabled in settings

Language packs downloaded on-device for offline voice recognition (Mozilla DeepSpeech as offline fallback)



7.4 Navigation Architecture

7.4.1 Information Architecture

The app uses a persistent bottom tab bar (5 tabs) as the primary navigation. Navigation depth is limited to 3 levels maximum:

Tab

Icon

Buyer Mode Destination

Seller Mode Destination

house-fill

Personalized feed: recommended listings, market prices, alerts

Dashboard: orders received, listings performance, earnings summary

magnifyingglass

Browse other sellers' listings for market intelligence

list-bullet

My orders, bookings, service requests, job applications

My listings, incoming orders, service requests, applicants

message-fill

In-app chat with sellers/providers, system notifications

In-app chat with buyers, support messages

Profile

person-fill

Buyer profile, saved items, wallet balance, settings

Seller profile, bank account, subscription, KYC status



Mode Switch Toggle: A persistent floating pill in the top navigation bar shows current mode ("Buyer" / "Seller" with colored indicator). Single tap switches mode instantly. No confirmation dialog ΓÇö mode switch is a UI change only, not a data operation. The toggle only appears for users who have both buyer and seller profiles activated.



7.5 Offline-First Architecture (Client-Side)

7.5.1 Local Database Schema (SQLite on Device)

Local Table

Data Stored

Sync Direction

Eviction Policy

local_listings_cache

Last 200 viewed/searched listings with full data

Server ΓåÆ Client

LRU eviction, max 200 entries

local_draft_listings

Unsent listing creations by this seller

Client ΓåÆ Server (on sync)

Delete after successful server sync

local_draft_orders

Unsent order placement attempts

Client ΓåÆ Server (on sync)

Delete after server confirms or user cancels

local_messages_cache

Last 50 messages per active conversation

Bidirectional

Keep 7 days, then evict

local_price_cache

Last-fetched price predictions per crop + location

Server ΓåÆ Client

Expire after 6 hours

local_profile_cache

Own user/seller profile data

Server ΓåÆ Client

Invalidate on profile update event

sync_queue

Pending write operations awaiting connectivity

Client ΓåÆ Server (on reconnect)

Retry up to 10 times, then surface to user

local_categories

All category data for all 4 marketplaces

Server ΓåÆ Client

Expire after 24 hours



Conflict Resolution Policy

For listing data (non-financial): Last-Write-Wins using server timestamp. For financial data (orders, payments): Server is always authoritative. Any client-side financial state that conflicts with server state is immediately overwritten by server state. The sync queue never attempts to re-apply a financial operation that the server has already rejected ΓÇö it surfaces the failure to the user for manual resolution.



8. Marketplace Feature Specifications

8.1 Crop Marketplace ΓÇö Full Feature Specification

8.1.1 Seller: Creating a Crop Listing (Step-by-Step Flow)

Step 1 ΓÇö Category Selection: Full-screen category picker with visual icons (photo of the crop, not abstract icons). Voice-searchable. Recent categories shown at top. 15 main categories with sub-categories.

Step 2 ΓÇö Crop Details: Crop name (voice or text), variety (autocomplete from curated database), quality grade (visual selector with descriptions), organic toggle, upload certification if organic.

Step 3 ΓÇö Quantity & Pricing: Quantity in seller's preferred unit (kg/quintal/maund ΓÇö unit selector). Price per unit input with LIVE comparison to current market price pulled from the AI service. Warning shown if price is > 20% above or > 30% below market. Is-negotiable toggle.

Step 4 ΓÇö Harvest & Availability: Date picker for harvest date and available-from date. Calendar view optimized for single-tap selection. Listing expiry set automatically to 30 days (editable).

Step 5 ΓÇö Photos: Camera integration with guided framing overlay. Minimum 1 photo required, up to 8. Automatic compression and WebP conversion client-side before upload. Rejected if image fails NSFW/relevance check (server-side ML classifier).

Step 6 ΓÇö Location: Pre-fills from seller's primary location. Option to pin on map. Shows estimated radius served by platform logistics partners.

Step 7 ΓÇö Delivery Options: Multi-select from self-pickup, platform logistics, custom delivery. If platform logistics selected, shows estimated logistics cost to buyer's district.

Step 8 ΓÇö Review & Publish: Final summary screen. Price intelligence widget shows: current market price, predicted price in 7 days, comparable active listings. Seller can adjust price before publishing. Estimated time for listing to go live (instant for verified sellers, < 30 min for unverified pending review).



8.1.2 Buyer: Search & Discovery Flow

Unified Search Bar: On the Explore tab, a prominent search bar with microphone icon. Query searches across crop name, variety, seller name, and tags simultaneously via Meilisearch typo-tolerant search.

Smart Filters (always visible as horizontal chip row below search):

Proximity: 10km / 25km / 50km / 100km / State / Country ΓÇö powered by PostGIS radius query

Category: Multi-select chip selector

Price: Range slider with min/max in local unit

Quality: Grade A / Organic / Premium checkboxes

Availability: Available now / Within 7 days / Within 30 days

Verified Sellers Only: Toggle

Sort Options: Nearest first (default) / Lowest price / Highest rated / Newest listings

Listing Detail Screen: Seller avatar + name + rating + distance + verified badge. Large image carousel. Price display with "Compare to market price" expandable widget. Harvest date, grade, quantity in bold. Map showing seller location (fuzzy ΓÇö not exact pin). Seller's other listings horizontal scroll. "Message Seller" and "Order Now" persistent bottom bar.



8.2 Equipment Rental Marketplace ΓÇö Full Feature Specification

8.2.1 Availability Calendar System

Real-time availability calendar is a core feature. Implementation:

Provider manages blocked/available dates in a visual calendar UI within their listing management screen

Calendar stored as a set of "blocked" date ranges in the equipment_availability_blocks table

On the renter-facing booking screen, unavailable dates are shown in grey and non-tappable

When a booking is confirmed, the booked dates are automatically added to the blocked ranges

Calendar updates are pushed to all active viewers of the listing via Supabase Realtime websocket subscriptions



8.2.2 GPS Tracking for High-Value Equipment

Provider can optionally enable GPS tracking by linking a supported GPS tracker (e.g., Tracki, LK506) via API integration

During an active rental, both provider and renter can view real-time location on an in-app map

Geo-fence: Provider sets an allowed operation zone. System alerts provider and platform if equipment exits zone

Location data is deleted 48 hours after rental completion (privacy compliance)



8.3 Rural Services Marketplace ΓÇö Full Feature Specification

8.3.1 Service Booking & Appointment Flow

For fixed-price services: Immediate booking with escrow payment. Calendar selector ΓåÆ Time slot selector ΓåÆ Confirm ΓåÆ Pay ΓåÆ Booking confirmed.

For quote-based services: Seeker submits a request with requirements and preferred dates. Provider receives notification and submits a quote within the app. Seeker reviews quote and accepts or negotiates via in-app chat. On acceptance, escrow payment initiated. No money changes hands until quote accepted.

Service Completion Verification: Provider marks service as "completed" with optional photo evidence upload. Seeker receives push notification: "Confirm [Provider Name] completed [service]?" with two-tap confirm or raise issue option. 48-hour auto-confirm if seeker is unresponsive (funds auto-released to provider).



8.4 Rural Job Marketplace ΓÇö Full Feature Specification

8.4.1 Job Seeker Profile Builder

Multi-step profile builder designed for users with no formal resume experience:

Step 1 ΓÇö Personal info: Name, location, preferred work radius

Step 2 ΓÇö Work experience: Visual timeline UI. "What work have you done?" Voice-input friendly. Pre-set category tiles (Farm labor, Construction, Driving, Teaching, Healthcare, etc.) with custom entry option

Step 3 ΓÇö Skills: Tag-based selector. 200+ pre-defined rural skills. Voice-add custom skills

Step 4 ΓÇö Availability: Calendar selector for available-from date. Full-time / Part-time / Weekend-only toggles

Step 5 ΓÇö Wage expectation: Per-day slider with market rate guidance shown

Step 6 ΓÇö Languages: Multi-select. Used to match job seekers to employers who prefer specific language communication

Step 7 ΓÇö Profile photo: Required for trust. Camera integration with face-detection crop guide



8.4.2 One-Tap Apply

The entire job application flow must complete in < 3 taps from the job listing screen:

Tap 1: "Apply Now" button on job listing

Tap 2: Review pre-filled application (profile data auto-populated), optional cover note via voice

Tap 3: "Submit Application" ΓÇö done

Application status tracked in real-time. Push notification when employer views application, invites for interview, or sends a message.



9. Escrow System ΓÇö Complete State Machine

9.1 State Machine Definition

State

Meaning

Who Can Trigger Transition From This State

Possible Next States

AWAITING_PAYMENT

Order created, payment not yet received

Buyer (pay) / System (auto-cancel after 30 min)

FUNDED, CANCELLED

FUNDED

Payment received and held in escrow

Seller (mark shipped/started) / Buyer (cancel within window)

SHIPPED, IN_PROGRESS, CANCELLATION_REQUESTED

SHIPPED

Buyer (confirm received) / System (auto-accept after 72h) / Buyer (reject)

DELIVERED, REJECTED

IN_PROGRESS

Service underway (Services marketplace)

Provider (mark completed) / Buyer (raise issue)

COMPLETED_PENDING, DISPUTED

Buyer confirmed receipt of goods

Buyer (accept / reject within inspection window)

ACCEPTED, REJECTED

COMPLETED_PENDING

Provider marked service complete

Buyer (confirm / raise issue within 48h)

ACCEPTED, DISPUTED

ACCEPTED

Buyer confirmed satisfaction

System (auto-trigger fund release)

RELEASING_FUNDS

RELEASING_FUNDS

Commission calculated, payout initiated

System (payout success/failure)

COMPLETED, PAYOUT_FAILED

COMPLETED

Final state ΓÇö funds released to seller

N/A (terminal state)

ΓÇö

REJECTED

Buyer rejected goods within inspection window

System (start 72h negotiation period)

IN_DISPUTE, REFUNDED (if seller agrees)

IN_DISPUTE

Formal dispute filed, funds frozen

Arbitrator (decide outcome)

REFUNDED, PARTIALLY_REFUNDED, COMPLETED

REFUNDED

Full refund issued to buyer

N/A (terminal state)

ΓÇö

PARTIALLY_REFUNDED

Split decision by arbitrator

N/A (terminal state)

ΓÇö

CANCELLED

Order cancelled before funding

N/A (terminal state)

ΓÇö

PAYOUT_FAILED

Payout to seller failed (bank error)

Finance team (retry) / System (auto-retry x3)

COMPLETED, MANUAL_REVIEW



9.2 Dispute Resolution Process

When a buyer rejects goods or raises an issue, the following structured process is initiated:

Hour 0: Buyer submits rejection with reason category (Not as described / Damaged / Short quantity / Did not arrive) and optional photo evidence. Status ΓåÆ REJECTED.

Hours 0ΓÇô72: Automated negotiation window. Seller notified with buyer's reason and evidence. In-app chat facilitated between buyer and seller. Seller can offer partial refund via the app. Buyer can accept partial refund or full resolution.

Hour 72 (if unresolved): Status ΓåÆ IN_DISPUTE. Case assigned to an internal support arbitrator. Funds remain frozen. Both parties can submit additional evidence via in-app evidence uploader.

Hours 72ΓÇô240 (10 days): Arbitrator reviews evidence, may request additional information via in-app messaging. Arbitrator makes final decision using the admin dashboard dispute resolution tool.

Decision: Arbitrator selects: Full release to seller / Full refund to buyer / Custom split (enters percentage). System executes the financial operation automatically. Both parties notified.

Appeal: Either party can escalate to a senior arbitrator within 48 hours of decision, providing new evidence not submitted earlier. Maximum one appeal per dispute.



10. AI & Machine Learning Integration

10.1 Crop Price Prediction ΓÇö Technical Architecture

10.1.1 Data Pipeline

The prediction engine is fed by a multi-source data pipeline ingesting five categories of data:

Data Source

Type

Ingestion Method

Update Frequency

Fields Used

Agmarknet / UPAg (India)

Government market prices

REST API + daily scraper fallback

Daily

commodity, market, min_price, max_price, modal_price, date

IMD Weather API

Weather forecasts

REST API

Every 6 hours

temperature, rainfall, humidity, district

NASA MODIS NDVI

Satellite vegetation index

GeoTIFF download + processing

Every 8 days

NDVI value per pixel, date, bounding box

Kisan Connect user data

Crowdsourced prices

Internal event stream

Real-time

crop, reported_price, location, date, user_reputation

Festival / holiday calendar

Demand shock events

Static + annual update

Annual

festival_name, date, affected_commodities, historical_demand_multiplier



10.1.2 Feature Engineering

Lag features: price(t-1), price(t-7), price(t-14), price(t-30) per commodity per market

Rolling statistics: 7-day mean, 7-day std dev, 30-day mean (captures seasonal volatility)

Weather features: 7-day forecast avg temperature, total rainfall forecast, humidity index

NDVI delta: Change in vegetation index from previous period (leading indicator of yield)

Festival proximity: Days to nearest high-demand festival, with commodity-specific demand weight

Supply shock indicator: Binary flag if recent weather event (flood/drought) detected in source region

Price momentum: Rolling 7-day price change percentage (captures trending markets)

Market connectivity: Distance from production region to nearest APMC market (affects price spread)



10.1.3 Model Architecture

The production model is an ensemble of three models, with predictions weighted by historical accuracy per commodity:

Model 1 ΓÇö Prophet: Facebook Prophet for interpretable baseline forecasts. Handles seasonality, trend, and holiday effects. Output: 30-day forecast. Used for stable, slow-moving commodities (rice, wheat, dal). Weight: 30%.

Model 2 ΓÇö LSTM: Bidirectional LSTM with attention mechanism. Input window: 60 days of lagged features. Hidden layers: 3 LSTM layers (128, 64, 32 units) + 2 Dense layers. Output: 7-day forecast. Trained per commodity cluster. Weight: 50%.

Model 3 ΓÇö XGBoost: Gradient boosted tree model on engineered features. Best for capturing non-linear relationships between weather, NDVI, and price. Output: 3-day forecast (highest short-term accuracy). Weight: 20%.



Retraining Schedule

Full model retrain: Weekly on Sunday 2 AM IST using previous 2 years of data. Incremental update: Daily fine-tuning on last 7 days of data. Automatic retrain trigger: If prediction MAE exceeds 15% of actual price over a 3-day rolling window. All models tracked in MLflow. New model replaces production only if validation MAPE improves by > 2%.



10.1.4 API Specification

Prediction endpoint:

GET /v1/ai/predictions/price

Query Parameters: crop_id (required), district (required), duration_days (7|14|30, default 7), language (for translated key_factors). Response includes: predictions array [{date, min_price_paisa, predicted_price_paisa, max_price_paisa, confidence_score}], trend_summary (plain text in user's language), key_factors (list of human-readable factors driving the prediction), comparable_markets (nearest 3 markets with their current prices), recommendation (SELL_NOW | HOLD_7_DAYS | HOLD_14_DAYS based on trend).



10.2 Recommendation Engine

10.2.1 Personalized Listing Recommendations

Algorithm: Collaborative filtering (user-item matrix factorization) combined with content-based filtering on listing attributes. Implemented using implicit feedback signals:

Listing view (+1 signal)

Listing save/wishlist (+3 signal)

Message sent to seller (+5 signal)

Order placed (+10 signal)

Review submitted post-purchase (+15 signal)

Cold-start problem for new users: Location-based popular listings in their district ΓåÆ category-based trending ΓåÆ global trending. Profile questionnaire during onboarding (3 questions: crop types interested, budget range, frequency of purchase) seeds initial recommendations.



10.3 AI-Powered Trust & Safety

Listing Image Classifier: TensorFlow Lite model running on-device to flag potentially irrelevant or inappropriate images before upload. Server-side model confirms. False positive rate < 1%.

Price Anomaly Detector: Z-score analysis comparing new listing price to last 30-day market price. Listings > 3╧â from mean held for review. Reduces fraudulent price-gouging and scam listings.

Fake Review Detector: NLP classifier trained on Indian language reviews to detect review farms (similar phrasing patterns, rapid submission, no prior transaction history). Flagged reviews withheld pending manual review.

Support Chatbot: Intent classification model (fine-tuned mBERT on rural domain support queries) handles 12 Indian languages. Resolves 70% of support queries without human agent. Escalates to human agent with full context summary for remaining 30%.



11. Payment Gateway Architecture

11.1 Gateway Abstraction Layer

All payment operations flow through the Payment Gateway Service, which is the only component that knows about specific payment gateway implementations. Upstream services (Order Service, Escrow Service) interact only with the abstraction layer via a unified internal API.

11.1.1 Payment Initiation Flow

Step 1: Order/Booking service calls POST /internal/payments/create-order with {amount_paisa, currency, buyer_id, reference_id, reference_type, metadata}

Step 2: Payment Service selects the optimal gateway based on the payment method selected by user and configured gateway priority routing table

Step 3: Gateway-specific order is created. Gateway order ID stored. Status ΓåÆ PENDING

Step 4: Client receives a gateway-agnostic payment_session_id and payment_url/deep_link

Step 5: User completes payment in their UPI app / bank app. Gateway sends webhook to Payment Service's dedicated webhook endpoint

Step 6: Webhook signature verified (HMAC-SHA256 with gateway-provided secret). Idempotency check via payment_reference against processed_webhooks table

Step 7: If payment successful: Transaction Service records COMPLETED event. Kafka publishes payment.success event. Escrow Service picks it up and moves to FUNDED.

Step 8: If payment failed: Kafka publishes payment.failed event. Order Service marks order as PAYMENT_FAILED. User receives push notification with retry option.

Payment Method

11.3 Payout Architecture

Seller payouts are processed through Razorpay Route (India) for automated split payments. The payout schedule is configurable per seller tier:

Seller Tier

Payout Schedule

Minimum Payout Amount

Processing Fee

Basic (Free)

Weekly (every Monday)

Γé╣100

Γé╣5 flat per payout

Standard (Γé╣199/month)

Twice weekly (Mon & Thu)

Γé╣50

Γé╣2 flat per payout

Pro (Γé╣499/month)

Daily (next business day)

Γé╣10

Zero

Enterprise (Γé╣999/month)

Instant (on escrow release)

Γé╣1

Zero



Payout Failure Handling: If a payout fails (invalid bank account, bank server down), the system retries 3 times with 30-minute intervals. After 3 failures, the seller is notified to update their bank details. Funds remain in the platform ledger, accruing to the next successful payout attempt. No funds are ever lost.

12.2 Critical Test Scenarios (Must Pass Before Launch)

Financial Integrity Tests

TC-FIN-001: Place order ΓåÆ Payment succeeds ΓåÆ Escrow funded ΓåÆ Seller ships ΓåÆ Buyer confirms ΓåÆ Funds released to seller (minus commission) ΓåÆ Review submitted. Verify all financial records balance.

TC-FIN-002: Place order ΓåÆ Payment succeeds ΓåÆ Buyer rejects ΓåÆ Dispute initiated ΓåÆ Arbitrator decides full refund ΓåÆ Buyer account credited ΓåÆ Seller ledger debited ΓåÆ Zero net position for platform.

TC-FIN-003: Duplicate webhook delivery ΓÇö verify idempotency prevents double-processing of the same payment event.

TC-FIN-004: Network timeout during payment initiation ΓÇö verify no orphaned payment records and order status correctly reverts to AWAITING_PAYMENT.

TC-FIN-005: Payout failure ΓÇö verify funds held safely, seller notified, no loss of funds in ledger.



Security Tests

TC-SEC-001: Attempt to access another user's order via direct API call ΓÇö verify 403 Forbidden.

TC-SEC-002: Attempt to modify escrow status via direct API call with buyer JWT ΓÇö verify rejection (only system can transition certain states).

TC-SEC-003: SQL injection via listing search query ΓÇö verify sanitization and parameterization.

TC-SEC-004: JWT token from one user used to place order as a different buyer ΓÇö verify rejection.

TC-SEC-005: Rapid-fire OTP attempts ΓÇö verify lockout after 3 failures.

13.2 Supply-Side Acquisition Strategy

The supply side (sellers/providers) is the hardest and most important side to acquire. A platform with rich supply automatically attracts demand. The following strategies prioritize supply onboarding:

Champion Farmer Program: 10 respected farmers per district given lifetime Pro subscription, co-creation input, and a branded "Kisan Connect Ambassador" certificate. Their testimonials (video recorded on their farms) are the primary marketing material.

FPO & Cooperative Partnerships: Partner with Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) to onboard entire groups at once. One FPO can bring 200ΓÇô2,000 farmer-members. Offer group subscription discounts.

Kisan Mitra (Field Agent) Network: Hire or partner with local educated youth (12th pass, smartphone literate) as Kisan Mitras. They physically visit farms, assist with listing creation, and earn commission per active seller onboarded and per transaction volume generated by their sellers.

Standalone Value First: Give sellers the AI price prediction tool and weather alerts for free with zero selling activity required. This creates habit and demonstrated value before asking for the first listing.

Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) Partnership: Engage with the 700+ government agricultural extension centers across India for official endorsement and farmer training sessions co-hosted with the platform.



13.3 Demand-Side Acquisition Strategy

First-Transaction Subsidy: Platform subsidizes Γé╣50ΓÇôΓé╣100 discount on each buyer's first transaction. Funded from the marketing budget, not from seller commission. This creates real transactions for the supply side to fulfill.

WhatsApp-First Discovery: Each listing generates a shareable WhatsApp card with price, photo, and a deep-link into the app. Sellers share their own listings organically on existing WhatsApp groups.

Referral Engine: Buyer refers another buyer ΓåÆ both get Γé╣25 platform credits on the referred user's first purchase. Implemented as a wallet credit, not cash ΓÇö drives repeat usage.

APMC / Mandi Presence: Physical booth presence at Agricultural Produce Market Committees (APMCs) during peak trading seasons. Farmers and traders are already at the mandi ΓÇö the app provides a digital equivalent with better price discovery.

15.2 Monitoring & Observability Stack

Application Performance Monitoring: Grafana Cloud (free tier ΓÇö 10K active series). Custom dashboards for each microservice: request rate, error rate, latency P50/P95/P99.

Error Tracking: Sentry (free tier ΓÇö 5K errors/month). Source maps uploaded. Alerts for new error types and error rate spikes. Mobile crash reporting via Sentry React Native SDK.

Distributed Tracing: OpenTelemetry SDK instrumentation on all services. Traces exported to Grafana Tempo (free self-hosted on Railway). Every request tagged with correlation_id propagated across all services.

Database Monitoring: Supabase dashboard for query performance. pg_stat_statements for slow query identification. Automated alert if any query P95 exceeds 500ms.

Business Metrics Dashboard: Custom Grafana dashboard tracking GMV, transaction success rate, escrow state distribution, active listings count, DAU/MAU ratio ΓÇö updated in real-time from Kafka event stream.

Uptime Monitoring: UptimeRobot (free tier) for external availability checks on API Gateway and critical endpoints. PagerDuty (free tier) for on-call alerting.

Log Management: Structured JSON logging on all services. Logs shipped to Grafana Loki (free tier). Log retention: 7 days hot, 30 days cold.



15.3 Disaster Recovery

Component

RTO (Recovery Time)

RPO (Data Loss Tolerance)

Strategy

< 1 hour

< 5 minutes

Supabase automated daily backups + WAL point-in-time recovery (paid tier)

< 1 minute

< 2 minutes

Acceptable (cache is cold-startable)

Redis RDB snapshots + auto-recovery on restart

DNS / Edge (Cloudflare)

< 1 minute

Zero

Cloudflare anycast network; automatic failover



16. Compliance, Legal & Data Privacy

16.1 India Personal Data Protection (PDPB) Compliance

Consent Framework: Granular, purpose-specific consent collected at onboarding. Consent can be withdrawn at any time from Settings ΓåÆ Privacy. Withdrawal triggers data minimization workflow within 30 days.

Data Minimization: Only data strictly necessary for the stated purpose is collected. No dark patterns to encourage over-sharing. Each optional data field clearly labeled as optional.

Right to Erasure: User can request full account deletion from Settings ΓåÆ Privacy ΓåÆ Delete Account. Deletion completes within 30 days. Financial records (required for 7 years under GST law) are anonymized, not deleted.

Data Localization: All user PII, transaction data, and KYC documents stored on servers physically located in India (Supabase Mumbai / AWS Mumbai). No PII transferred outside India without explicit consent.

Privacy by Design: Privacy impact assessment (PIA) conducted before any new feature that processes personal data. DPO (Data Protection Officer) role designated from day one.



16.2 Financial Compliance

KYC Requirements: Sellers processing > Γé╣50,000/month require Aadhaar + PAN verification (RBI guidelines on payment aggregators). KYC status displayed publicly on seller profile.

TDS Deduction: For sellers with annual payouts > Γé╣30,000, platform deducts TDS at 5% (Section 194-O of Income Tax Act) and issues Form 16C. Automated TDS calculation in Commission Service.

GST: Platform is registered for GST. Commission charged to sellers is inclusive of 18% GST. Monthly GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B filing via Clear GST integration.

Payment Aggregator License: Razorpay and PhonePe hold RBI Payment Aggregator licenses. Platform operates as a merchant on these licensed gateways, not as a PA itself ΓÇö no RBI PA license required for the platform during MVP phase.

Escrow Account: Dedicated nodal account at a scheduled commercial bank (HDFC / SBI) as required for marketplace escrow operations under RBI guidelines.



16.3 Consumer Protection

Return & Refund Policy: Clearly displayed in user's preferred language before every transaction. 72-hour inspection window for crop orders. Escrow system enforces policy automatically.

Grievance Redressal: Dedicated Grievance Officer per Consumer Protection E-commerce Rules 2020. Response within 48 hours. Resolution within 30 days. Contact details published in-app and on website.

Prohibited Items Policy: Clearly defined list of items not tradeable on the platform (controlled substances, weapons, stolen goods). ML-based content moderation on all listings at creation time.

18.2 AI Pair-Programming Guidelines (For AI Assistants on the Team)

Instructions for AI Developers

When an AI assistant is contributing code to this codebase: (1) Always read the relevant module's README.md before writing code. (2) All financial calculations must be in BIGINT paisa ΓÇö never use floats. (3) All database queries must use the ORM (Prisma) ΓÇö no raw SQL in application code. (4) All new endpoints must have a Zod schema for request validation. (5) Every new function must have at least one Jest unit test. (6) Check the escrow state machine diagram before touching any escrow-related code. (7) Follow the established error response format: {success: false, error: {code, message, details}}. (8) All new environment variables must be documented in .env.example and in the service's README.



19. Appendix: API Reference Highlights

19.1 Core API Conventions

Base URL: https://api.kisanconnect.in/v1

Authentication: Bearer {JWT} header on all authenticated endpoints

Content-Type: application/json on all requests and responses

Pagination: Cursor-based. Response includes {data: [], cursor: "opaque_string", has_more: bool}

Error format: {success: false, error: {code: "LISTING_NOT_FOUND", message: "...", http_status: 404}}

Success format: {success: true, data: {...}, meta: {request_id, timestamp}}

Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset on every response

This document represents the complete product vision and engineering specification for Kisan Connect. It is a living document ΓÇö each section should be reviewed and updated as the product evolves, but the core principles (offline-first, trust-by-default, financial integrity, accessibility-first) must be treated as constitutional ΓÇö changes require explicit product leadership approval.
